---DECISIONS HEAD---
# Decisions

Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape — internally inconsistent. Picked the conservative reading: ship the seam (`credentials:store-blob:*`) without churning all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 reshapes when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` actually needs `kind`. | Reshape now per Section 3 (rejected: ~600 LOC of churn across credential-proxy + mcp-client + CLI for no Phase 1b benefit) |
| 2026-04-28 | `@ax/credentials-store-db` (Phase 1b) delegates to `storage:get` / `storage:set` rather than owning its own table + migrations | Design Section 3 says "owns the credentials table + Kysely migrations, dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" — but `@ax/database-sqlite` doesn't exist (only postgres has the hook), and the dedicated table earns its weight only with Phase 3 columns (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata`). The seam is still real: vault / KMS backends register `credentials:store-blob:*` against a different store. | Build `@ax/database-sqlite` first (rejected: bigger than Phase 1b); have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (rejected: deferred — wait until Phase 3 schema needs it) |
| 2026-04-29 | Phase A spike confirms D1 (HOME-redirect): runner-owned-sessions design moves forward unchanged | Set HOME=tempdir, ran `query()` one-shot then `query({resume})` — sessionId reattached, magic phrase recalled verbatim. Resume costs ~10× less than turn 1 (validates "replay is wasteful"). Two follow-ups for Phase C: (1) workspace persistence ignore-list keeps only `.claude/projects/`, drops `.claude.json` + backups + policy-limits; (2) `runner:read-transcript` uses sessionId glob, not encoded-cwd reconstruction (SDK uses `realpath(cwd)`, breaks on macOS `/var` → `/private/var`). | Bind-mount `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>` to a workspace path (rejected: more sandbox-specific plumbing, doesn't generalize to k8s — see design D1) |
| 2026-04-29 | No v1→v2 schema migrations in ax-next, ever | ax-next is greenfield with no production data to preserve. The "forward-only via future v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" rule in `conversations_v1` was inherited from a different mental model. ALTER TABLE in place is correct now AND for every future schema change. The `_v1` suffix on existing tables (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`, etc.) is now just a stable identifier, not a version pointer. Confirmed by Vinay 2026-04-29 during Phase B planning. | Maintain v1 → v2 split for breaking changes (rejected: greenfield, no rows to migrate; speculative versioning earns nothing) |
| 2026-05-21 | Finding 3 facade lives in `@ax/core` (`registerWorkspaceApplyFacade`); backends rename their raw hook to `workspace:apply-internal`; callers of `workspace:apply` are untouched | Facade logic (fire pre-apply → call internal → fire applied) is backend-agnostic, so core is the principled single home (a future GCS backend reuses it). Renaming the *registration* (not the call) means every existing `call('workspace:apply')` site routes through the facade with zero churn — the cleanest way to make the policy chokepoint unbypassable. `filterToPolicy` relocates to core (only depends on the `FileChange` core type) so the facade can policy-filter pre-apply identically to commit-notify. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-finding3-workspace-apply-facade.md`. | Per-backend duplicated facade (rejected: violates one-source-of-truth); facade in `@ax/workspace-git-core` (rejected: not backend-agnostic — GCS won't import it); also route `apply-bundle`/commit-notify through the facade (rejected: commit-notify already fires both hooks, not a bypasser; out of finding-3 scope) |
| 2026-05-21 | New `yolo-ship` skill is an **orchestrator that sequences existing skills**, not a re-implementation | The brainstorm/design/implement/ship phases map 1:1 onto superpowers:brainstorming / writing-plans / subagent-driven-development / finishing-a-development-branch + commit-push-pr. The skill adds only the autonomy contract + the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop, and cross-references the rest by name (no @ links, per writing-skills). | Write a self-contained monolith re-explaining each phase (rejected: duplicates skills that already exist + evolve independently; bloats context) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` keeps context low via **subagent-driven implementation + Explore for reads**; orchestrator holds only plan + memory + summaries | User's hard cap is < 300–400K tokens across all three phases. Per-task implementation subagents give ~50–100× savings; codebase exploration goes through Explore so file dumps never hit the orchestrator. | One long-running orchestrator agent doing everything inline (rejected: just relocates the context blowup); fully delegate brainstorm+design to a subagent too (rejected: orchestrator's judgment + memory are most valuable in those phases) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` runs brainstorming in **self-answering mode** and logs decisions instead of asking the user | User instruction: "don't ask questions unless absolutely necessary — trust your recommendations as long as you document them in memory." Brainstorming is normally interactive; here the orchestrator generates the questions, answers from code/memory/conventions, and records each in decisions.md. AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks good… you are done") — no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |
| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300–400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
| 2026-05-21 | `@ax/web-tools` impl: branch the worktree off `feat/web-tools` HEAD (not main); PR base = `main`, carrying the design-spec + impl-plan doc commits into the PR | The two doc commits (497fb157, 450153bb) are part of this feature and belong in the same PR. Branching off main would orphan them. | Open PR against feat/web-tools (rejected: CodeRabbit only reviews PRs based on main); separate docs PR (rejected: needless split) |
| 2026-05-21 | Verified all 14 load-bearing plan claims via one Explore agent before implementing — grounding TRUE (core exports, `PluginError` code union incl. `invalid-payload`/`init-failed`, host-exec contract = full ToolCall + `{output}` wrap, `^0.91.1`, preset insertion points) | Plan itself flagged the `memory-search` `input.query` bug; don't trust signatures blindly. Item-14 Explore self-contradiction ("HookBus unwraps the payload") is WRONG — item 6 is ground truth: web-tools reads `call.input`, mirroring mcp-client, NOT memory-search. | Trust the plan as-written (rejected: cheap to verify; a wrong signature would fail a subagent mid-chain) |
| 2026-05-21 | Implement via ~6 **sequential** subagents grouped by coherent file-unit (scaffold / url-guard+anthropic-client / two tool executors / plugin+exports / canary / preset-wiring), not strict 1-per-12-tasks | Plan gives complete code per step; grouping keeps TDD checkpoints + the plan's per-step commits intact while cutting orchestration round-trips. Subagents share the worktree fs, so they MUST run sequentially (each builds on prior commits), never parallel. | Strict 1-subagent-per-task (rejected: 12 round-trips for a paint-by-numbers plan); inline (rejected: blows orchestrator context budget) |
| 2026-05-21 | `web_extract` output is `{ url, title?, text }` — drop the `truncated` field the design spec listed | Impl plan (Task 4 + Task 12 Step 3) resolves the spec/plan discrepancy by dropping `truncated` and syncing the spec: `max_content_tokens` already caps content server-side and a bare boolean adds no actionable signal for the agent. | Keep `truncated` (rejected: no consumer; would require tracking server-side clipping we don't observe) |
| 2026-05-21 | Scope the web-tools PR to web-tools only: rebase `feat/web-tools-impl --onto origin/main`, dropping the 2 unrelated commits `b3c87929` (auth-better baseURL) + `494b9e7c` (channel-web pill spacing) that rode on `feat/web-tools` | Reviewer-disciplined / scoped-PR convention (Vinay's documented style): a "web-tools" PR shouldn't carry unrelated fixes — they'd muddy CodeRabbit's review + the PR narrative. Branch lacked only PR #120 with zero file overlap, so the rebase is conflict-free. The 2 dropped commits are PRESERVED on the untouched original `feat/web-tools` branch; flagged in TODO + final report so the user can ship them separately. | Keep them in the PR (rejected: scope creep, unrelated review surface); ask the user (rejected: inferable from convention; commits preserved either way) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE: worktree branched **fresh from origin/main** (drops the 2 unpushed local commits f1e2deb7 channel-web spacing + c168fb8c auth baseURL — same pair as entry 27, recommitted) for a clean PR diff | Same reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention as entry 27; those 2 fixes don't intersect the title-SSE files. They stay on local `main` for the user to push separately. | Branch from local HEAD (rejected: PR diff would carry the 2 unrelated fixes) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE design (user-approved + autonomous): **per-user** route `GET /api/chat/title-events` (one connection covers all sidebar rows); fire `conversations:title-updated` from the **set-title write point** (one source of truth, covers future rename UI); client **updates the row in place** via new `session-store.applyTitle` + **bumpVersion()-on-connect resync** (no server replay buffer); mount the consumer in **AppContent** not Sidebar (Sidebar unmounts when admin panel opens). Additive — existing client poll untouched. Plan docs allowlisted in `.gitignore` (project default ignores `docs/plans/*`). | Per-user matches the sidebar's need; set-title is the only title writer; direct-update avoids a refetch per frame while onOpen-resync covers disconnect gaps; AppContent is the stable post-auth shell. In-process bus (single-replica) matches the chat-SSE precedent — cross-replica deferred (design I6). | Per-conversation SSE (rejected: N connections); fire from conversation-titles (rejected: not the write point); refetch-per-frame (rejected: wasteful); replace the poll (rejected: user chose additive) |
| 2026-05-22 | Credentialed-CLI/git-auth (yolo-ship of `docs/plans/2026-05-22-credentialed-cli-tools-and-git-auth-design.md`): **`GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` is already stamped** in both `sandbox-k8s/pod-spec.ts` (`gitParanoidEnv`) and `sandbox-subprocess/open-session.ts` — design §4.3/§4.6 asked to add it; cut to a **regression assertion** instead of re-stamping | Verified by Explore against current code: both sandbox providers already set it (value `'0'`) and `proxy-startup.ts` forwards `GIT_*` by prefix (`ENV_ALLOWLIST_PREFIXES=['GIT_','LC_']`). Re-adding would be a no-op edit. YAGNI cut per [[feedback_yagni_check_in_plans]]; a guard test catches accidental removal. | Re-stamp it (rejected: already present, dead edit); skip even the assertion (rejected: it's load-bearing for B's fail-fast, cheap to lock in) |
| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project B Basic-auth transform uses a **per-request re-arming HTTP/1.1 framing state machine** in the MITM client→upstream path, not single-head-per-connection as design §4.2 literally describes | git smart-HTTP reuses ONE CONNECT tunnel for `GET /info/refs?service=git-upload-pack` (no body) **then** `POST /git-upload-pack` (body), and with creds in the URL libcurl sends `Authorization: Basic` **preemptively on both**. Single-head buffering would transform the GET but leave the POST's base64 placeholder corrupted → fetch fails after refs succeed. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |
| 2026-05-22 | B canary parity: run the canary scan on the **decoded** `user:pass` inside the transform, before re-encode | Canary scanning today is inline `chunk.includes(token)` on raw decrypted bytes (`listener.ts`), not a registry function — a base64-wrapped canary in a Basic blob would slip past it once we start decoding. Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |
| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project D targets **`@ax/skills-parser`** (`src/manifest.ts` + `src/capabilities.ts`), not `@ax/skills` | Explore confirmed `@ax/skills/src/manifest.ts` is a one-line re-export of `@ax/skills-parser`'s `parseSkillManifest`; the real grammar + hand-rolled validation + `ManifestCode` union live in `@ax/skills-parser`. Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |
| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |
| 2026-05-22 | uv/uvx added to the agent image via `COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:<pinned-tag> /uv /uvx /usr/local/bin/` with a **version tag** (digest-pin deferred); `python3` from Debian bookworm via the existing apt line | Matches design §5.5/§5.7 and the repo's existing ca-certificates/tini "pin by version now, digest-pin as a tracked follow-up" posture. uv is a static Rust binary (lightweight, no runtime deps); reusing system `python3` means `uvx` never downloads a Python build (no extra egress host). The implementing subagent picks the latest stable uv tag and the image smoke test (`uv`, `uvx`, `python3` present + executable as `axagent`) verifies it. | pip+venv management by us (rejected: design explicitly avoids — uvx is ephemeral-env on demand); download python-build-standalone (rejected: extra egress host, system python3 suffices for MVP); digest-pin now (rejected: follow-up alongside the existing tini/ca-certs pin TODO) |
| 2026-05-23 | Python-venv yolo-ship: work **in-place on `feat/python-venv-pip` off main**, not a separate worktree | `docs/plans/*` is gitignored (`.gitignore:32`) so the plan file lives only in this checkout; a fresh worktree would need the plan copied in + a full `pnpm install` (node_modules already present here). Only uncommitted changes are untracked/gitignored (decisions.md, `.claude/prompts/`, etc.) which targeted `git add` won't sweep in — isolation is already effective. | Separate git worktree per yolo-ship default (rejected: gitignored plan not carried + costly reinstall for no real isolation gain) |

content.text: Now I understand the task. Let me read the worktree feedback and the docker-build-cache memory (relevant since this touches the runner/host boundary), and set up the isolated worktree as Phase 0 requires.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree
3	description: "shared working tree + concurrent background agents → do isolated commit work in a git worktree, not on a branch in the shared checkout"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: feedback
7	  originSessionId: 3c3833f9-b6d8-4fa4-8cd7-67eb7050d87d
8	---
9	
10	This repo's single working tree is frequently shared with concurrent background agents
11	(e.g. yolo-ship runs) that `git checkout` / cherry-pick mid-task. On 2026-05-23, while I was
12	building the chat-qa-sweep skill on branch `feat/chat-qa-sweep-skill`, another agent doing
13	python-venv work switched the checkout out from under me (→ `feat/python-venv-pip`), and my
14	committed-but-gitignored design doc (`docs/plans/*`) vanished from the working tree on the
15	switch. The user's fix: "move all your work into a new worktree."
16	
17	**Why:** the checkout's HEAD is global — another actor's `git checkout` moves yours too.
18	Untracked files survive a branch switch (so `.claude/skills/**`, which is NOT gitignored,
19	was safe); a committed file under a gitignored path (`docs/plans/*`) does NOT reappear
20	unless its branch is checked out, so it looked lost until I `git show <sha>:path`-restored
21	it.
22	
23	**How to apply:** for any multi-step work that will produce commits, isolate it in a git
24	worktree up front — `EnterWorktree` (branches fresh off origin/main into
25	`.claude/worktrees/<name>`, switches the session in) or `git worktree add`. Untracked files
26	do NOT travel into a new worktree, so copy them in from the original checkout by absolute
27	path, then commit (force-add gitignored `docs/plans/*`; `.claude/skills/**` adds normally).
28	Leave the shared checkout exactly as found. Related: [[feedback_check_local_commits_before_dispatching]],
29	[[feedback_workspace_lint_stale_worktree_noise]], [[feedback_decisions_md_stays_uncommitted]].

file.content:
---
name: feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree
description: "shared working tree + concurrent background agents → do isolated commit work in a git worktree, not on a branch in the shared checkout"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 3c3833f9-b6d8-4fa4-8cd7-67eb7050d87d
---

This repo's single working tree is frequently shared with concurrent background agents
(e.g. yolo-ship runs) that `git checkout` / cherry-pick mid-task. On 2026-05-23, while I was
building the chat-qa-sweep skill on branch `feat/chat-qa-sweep-skill`, another agent doing
python-venv work switched the checkout out from under me (→ `feat/python-venv-pip`), and my
committed-but-gitignored design doc (`docs/plans/*`) vanished from the working tree on the
switch. The user's fix: "move all your work into a new worktree."

**Why:** the checkout's HEAD is global — another actor's `git checkout` moves yours too.
Untracked files survive a branch switch (so `.claude/skills/**`, which is NOT gitignored,
was safe); a committed file under a gitignored path (`docs/plans/*`) does NOT reappear
unless its branch is checked out, so it looked lost until I `git show <sha>:path`-restored
it.

**How to apply:** for any multi-step work that will produce commits, isolate it in a git
worktree up front — `EnterWorktree` (branches fresh off origin/main into
`.claude/worktrees/<name>`, switches the session in) or `git worktree add`. Untracked files
do NOT travel into a new worktree, so copy them in from the original checkout by absolute
path, then commit (force-add gitignored `docs/plans/*`; `.claude/skills/**` adds normally).
Leave the shared checkout exactly as found. Related: [[feedback_check_local_commits_before_dispatching]],
[[feedback_workspace_lint_stale_worktree_noise]], [[feedback_decisions_md_stays_uncommitted]].

11

file.content:
---
name: docker-build-cache-runner-fixes
description: "When verifying a runner-code fix in the kind cluster, force a fresh docker build or grep the compiled output inside the image before testing — incremental builds can silently reuse a pre-fix layer."
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 72f92c44-7a1e-4afa-b923-f91812ea856a
---

When the fix you're verifying in `ax-next-dev` is **runner-code** (anything in `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/`), don't trust an incremental `docker build`. The image's compiled `main.js` can silently retain a pre-fix copy even though you're building from `main` with the fix merged.

**Why:** Docker layer caching keyed on filesystem state can decide the runner-bake layer is "the same" and reuse it. The host's grep of source on disk passes, but the bundled output the runner pod actually executes is the stale one.

**How to apply:** Before drawing conclusions from "but the fix is in!", verify the compiled output inside the image:

```bash
docker run --rm ax-next/agent:dev grep -A8 "event('event.turn-end'" \
  /opt/ax-next/host/node_modules/.pnpm/@ax+agent-claude-sdk-runner@file+packages+agent-claude-sdk-runner/node_modules/@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner/dist/main.js
```

If the fix isn't there, `docker build --no-cache -t ax-next/agent:dev -f container/agent/Dockerfile .` and retry. Adds ~2 min to the build but saves the ~5 min round-trip of "deploy → curl → wait → confirm nothing changed → realize the image is stale → rebuild".

Host-only (non-runner) fixes ride the fast loop's hostPath mount — see `[[k8s-acceptance-loop]]` — and don't hit this problem.

input.prompt:
I'm fixing a bug in the ax-next monorepo (cwd is a worktree at /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.claude/worktrees/fault-a-sse-turn-error, same code as main). 

THE BUG: When the serving runner pod is killed mid-turn (k8s pod deleted), the host logs `pod_exited code:143` + `pod_killed`, but the client SSE stream (`/api/chat/stream/:reqId`, HTTP 200) never receives an error or `done` frame. So the client's "Thinking…" spinner + Stop button persist indefinitely (~155s observed, no console error). The fix needs the host to emit a turn-error frame to the SSE on mid-turn `pod_exited`/runner-disconnect so the UI flips to error+retry.

I need a thorough map of the relevant code. Do a VERY THOROUGH search across these areas and report conclusions (file:line citations, function names, data shapes — NOT full file dumps):

1. **The SSE stream endpoint** `/api/chat/stream/:reqId` (or similar). Where is it registered? What plugin/package? What frames does it emit (event types like `delta`, `done`, `error`, `tool`, etc.)? What is the exact wire format of each frame? Where does it currently emit `done`? Does it have any error-frame path at all? How does it know when a turn is complete?

2. **The chat orchestrator** (`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts` and siblings). Trace the flow: client POST a message → `agent:invoke` → sandbox handle creation → streaming deltas back to SSE → `chat:turn-end`. How are streamed deltas pushed to the SSE (event bus? a per-reqId emitter/subject?)? What is the `reqId` → stream plumbing? How does the orchestrator currently detect/handle a sandbox/runner that exits or disconnects mid-turn? Look for `pod_exited`, `pod_killed`, `handle.exited`, `handle.kill`, runner disconnect, abnormal exit, exit code 143/SIGTERM handling.

3. **The sandbox handle / k8s provider** (`packages/sandbox-k8s/` and any `sandbox-*`). What does `handle.exited` resolve to? Is there an event/promise that fires when the pod dies unexpectedly (vs a clean turn-end)? How does the orchestrator distinguish "runner exited cleanly after turn-end" from "runner died mid-turn"? Where are the logs `pod_exited` and `pod_killed` emitted, and with what context?

4. **The keepalive reaper** (PR #124 — `warmSessions`, `armReapTimer`, `handle.kill`, `idle-timeout`). When the host force-kills a wedged runner, what happens to any in-flight turn/SSE? When a turn is genuinely in-flight (not idle) and the pod dies externally, what code path runs?

5. **Client side** (`packages/channel-web/`). Find `AgentStatus` component and the SSE consumer (likely in `runtime.tsx` or a transport file). What SSE event types does the client handle? What makes the spinner show "Thinking…" vs flip to an error+retry state? What frame/event would the client need to receive to show error+retry? Is there a `.msg-error` / Toast / error surface it already supports? How does the client currently handle SSE close without a `done`? (Faults B/D in the same sweep show the client synthesizes a finish on a done-less close but surfaces nothing — find that code.)

6. **Existing tests** for the orchestrator SSE path and runner-exit handling — where do they live, what do they assert, what test helpers/mocks exist for the sandbox handle and SSE? I'll need to write a regression test.

7. The `agent:invoke` hook contract and the SSE-emitter hook (if streaming goes through a hook). What does the deferred/promise resolve to on a failed turn today — does `agent:invoke` reject, resolve with an error kind, or hang? Look for an outcome union like `{kind:'complete'|'cancelled'|'error'|...}`.

Report a concise structured map. I especially need: (a) the exact place a turn-error SSE frame should be emitted on mid-turn runner death, (b) the existing SSE frame format so a new error frame matches it, (c) what the client needs to receive, (d) where the regression test goes.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with the findings.

Based on my thorough search of the ax-next codebase, here is a structured map of the SSE streaming, chat orchestrator, sandbox handling, and relevant code paths for implementing the mid-turn runner death → error-frame fix:

## **1. The SSE Stream Endpoint (`/api/chat/stream/:reqId`)**

**Files:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts` (SSE handler + buffer mgmt)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/types.ts` (frame type definitions)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts` (route registration)

**Key Facts:**
- **Endpoint:** `GET /api/chat/stream/:reqId` (line 132, sse.ts)
- **Handler:** `createSseHandler()` (line 79-304, sse.ts) — wires 4 subscriptions:
  1. `chat:stream-chunk` → emits content deltas to client
  2. `chat:phase` → emits out-of-band status ("Starting sandbox…")
  3. `chat:turn-end` → emits done frame + cleanup
  4. Keepalive comment (`:[\n\n]` every 25s)
  
**Current Frame Format** (`SseFrame` union, types.ts:64-67):
```typescript
type SseFrame =
  | StreamChunk  // { reqId, kind: 'text'|'thinking'|'tool-use'|'tool-result', ...payload }
  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
  | { reqId: string; done: true }
```

**Wire Format:** Each frame is `data: ${JSON.stringify(frame)}\n\n` (sse.ts:86)

**Done-frame emission:** Line 275, sse.ts — fires on `chat:turn-end` subscriber match (by `ctx.conversationId`), then closes stream (line 279).

**Error-frame path TODAY:** None. No error variant exists in `SseFrame` union. Stream simply closes when `handle.exited` resolves.

---

## **2. Chat Orchestrator (Streaming Deltas → Outcome Resolution)**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`

**Flow:**
- **`runAgentInvoke()`** (line 584-1427):
  1. `chat:start` veto (line 589)
  2. `agents:resolve` ACL (line 615)
  3. J6 Routing decision (line 658-816): route to existing session or open fresh?
  4. Fresh-spawn path (line 818-1427):
     - `proxy:open-session` (line 1067)
     - Register waiter deferred (line 1145)
     - `sandbox:open-session` (line 1186) — returns `handle` with `.exited` promise
     - Enqueue user message (line 1284)
     - **Await chat:end OR sandbox exit OR timeout** (line 1338-1365)
     - Return `AgentOutcome` (line 1355)
  5. Routed path (line 705-816): same flow but reuses existing sandbox, no handle ownership

**Sandbox Exit Handling** (line 1338-1351):
```typescript
handle.exited
  .then(() => {
    if (!deferred.settled) {
      resolvedByChatEndSubscriber = false;
      deferred.resolve({
        kind: 'terminated',
        reason: 'sandbox-exit-before-chat-end',
      });
    }
  })
  .catch(() => {/* swallow */});
```

**Critical Gap:** When `handle.exited` resolves mid-turn (pod dies, no chat:end fire), orchestrator resolves its deferred with `terminated` outcome but **DOES NOT fire any SSE error frame**. The SSE stream just hangs waiting for `chat:turn-end`.

**Waiters tracking:** Map keyed by `ctx.reqId` (not sessionId) per line 473. When orchestrator synthesizes a `terminated` outcome and fires `chat:end` (line 1374), the event is broadcast but no frame reaches the SSE stream.

---

## **3. Sandbox Handle / K8s Provider (Pod Exit Detection)**

**Files:** 
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/sandbox-k8s/src/lifecycle.ts` (watchPodExit)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/sandbox-k8s/src/open-session.ts` (pod lifecycle + handle creation)

**Handle Structure** (open-session.ts:155-158):
```typescript
interface OpenSessionHandle {
  kill(): Promise<void>;
  exited: Promise<ExitInfo>;  // { code, signal, reason }
}
```

**Exit Detection** (`watchPodExit`, lifecycle.ts:162-221):
- Polls `readNamespacedPod()` every 250ms until phase reaches terminal state
- Returns `ExitInfo` with code/signal/reason (line 140-151)
- Distinguishes: `pod-gone`, `OOMKilled`, `Error`, `Evicted`, `DeadlineExceeded`, etc. (line 200)
- **Logs:** `pod_exited` event at line 209 with `{ phase, code, reason, containerReason, podReason }`

**Log Entry Example** (from bug description): `pod_exited code:143 + pod_killed`
- Code 143 = SIGTERM (128 + 15)
- Reason would be `pod_killed` if k8s deleted it externally

---

## **4. How Streamed Deltas Reach SSE**

**Path:**
1. Runner emits `event.stream-chunk` POST to `/ipc/event/stream-chunk` (IPC server listener in presets)
2. IPC server fires `chat:stream-chunk` hook event with `StreamChunk` payload
3. Channel-web's buffer-fill subscriber captures it (plugin.ts:97-101)
4. **Per-connection** SSE subscriber listens and emits (sse.ts:240-250):
   ```typescript
   deps.bus.subscribe<StreamChunk>('chat:stream-chunk', chunkSubKey, async (_ctx, payload) => {
     if (payload.reqId !== reqId) return undefined;  // filter by reqId
     safeWrite(payload);
     return undefined;
   });
   ```
5. `safeWrite()` JSON-encodes frame and writes `data: ...\n\n` to stream

**ReqId as join key:** Server-minted at POST time (routes-chat.ts:402), stamped onto every chunk by runner, matched by SSE handler.

---

## **5. Client-Side SSE Consumption**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts`

**Parser:** `processResponseStream()` (line 356-544)
- Splits on `\n\n`, parses each `data: {...}` JSON line
- **Emission policy:**
  - `{ reqId, text, kind: 'text'|'thinking' }` → emits `text-delta` UIMessageChunk
  - `{ reqId, phase }` → side-channel: `agentStatusActions.set(label)` (line 451-452)
  - `{ reqId, done: true }` → `closeOpen()` + emit `finish` chunk + set `finished=true` (line 441-445)
  - **Stream close without done frame** (line 533-540): `flush()` synthesizes `finish` — **client sees finish but NO error signal**

**UI Layer** (`AgentStatus.tsx`, lines 34-122):
- Modes: `'hidden'` (collapsed), `'working'` (blue dot, breathing), `'error'` (red dot, persistent)
- Error mode shows retry button (via `agentStatusActions.error(text, { retry, dismiss })`)
- **Gap:** No code path calls `.error()` on SSE stream failure; only side-effect is synthetic finish

**Agent-Status Store** (`agent-status-store.ts`):
- `agentStatusActions.error(text, opts)` sets mode='error' + text + retry/dismiss callbacks (line 106-117)
- When user clicks retry, custom handlers (registered by runtime) re-queue the turn

---

## **6. Current Test Coverage**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts`

**Relevant Tests:**
- Line 364-406: "sandbox exits before chat:end" — orchestrator resolves `terminated` outcome, fires `chat:end`
- Line 408-456: Parametrized sweep: start-reject, queue-fail, open-fail, sandbox-exit scenarios
- **Gap:** No test for SSE stream still open when sandbox exits; no test for error-frame emission

---

## **7. Key Data Shapes & Flow**

**Agent:invoke → agent:invoke outcome:**
```typescript
type AgentOutcome = 
  | { kind: 'complete'; messages: UIMessage[] }
  | { kind: 'terminated'; reason: string; error?: unknown }
```
(Routes through `chat:end` event, keyed by sessionId for resolution.)

**ChannelWeb route dispatch** (routes-chat.ts:507-518):
```typescript
void bus.call<AgentInvokeInput, unknown>('agent:invoke', agentInvokeCtx, { message })
  .catch((err) => { /* log, don't await */ });
res.status(202).json({ conversationId, reqId });  // early return
```
Fire-and-forget; SSE stream already open on client side.

---

## **8. CRITICAL CODE LOCATIONS FOR THE FIX**

### **(a) Where to emit the error frame:**

**`orchestrator.ts` line 1338-1351** (`handle.exited` resolution):
Currently resolves deferred with `terminated` outcome. **NEW CODE:** also emit an error SSE frame via `bus.fire('chat:turn-error', ctx, { reqId, reason })` or similar.

**OR (better):**
**`orchestrator.ts` line 1373-1375** (failure path that synthesizes chat:end):
When `!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber`, fire both `chat:turn-error` AND `chat:end`.

### **(b) Hook to emit error frame on SSE:**

**`channel-web/src/server/plugin.ts` line 80 (subscribes array):**
Add `'chat:turn-error'` to the subscribes list.

**`channel-web/src/server/sse.ts` (new code):**
Add a subscriber for `chat:turn-error` (like the existing turn-end one, lines 264-285):
```typescript
deps.bus.subscribe<{ reqId?: string; reason?: string }>(
  'chat:turn-error',
  turnErrorSubKey,
  async (ctx, payload) => {
    if (payload.reqId !== reqId) return undefined;
    safeWrite({ reqId, error: payload.reason ?? 'unknown' });
    deps.buffer.evictReqId(reqId);
    cleanup();
    try { stream.close(); } catch { }
    return undefined;
  },
);
```

### **(c) New SseFrame variant:**

**`channel-web/src/server/types.ts` line 64-67:**
Add error variant:
```typescript
export type SseFrame =
  | StreamChunk
  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
  | { reqId: string; done: true }
  | { reqId: string; error: string };  // NEW
```

### **(d) Client-side error handler:**

**`channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts` line 440-479:**
In the frame-parsing loop, add:
```typescript
if ('error' in frame && typeof frame.error === 'string') {
  closeOpen(controller);
  // Emit an error chunk so the runtime can show error UI.
  // The AI SDK doesn't have a native error-chunk type, so we
  // emit a tool-output-error or use metadata to signal error.
  controller.enqueue({
    type: 'finish',
    finishReason: 'error',  // if AI SDK supports it
    // else: use metadata piggyback or a custom chunk type
  });
  finished = true;
  return;
}
```

**AND in the runtime hook** (that watches the stream chunks):
When a finish with `finishReason === 'error'` arrives, call:
```typescript
agentStatusActions.error('Runner died unexpectedly — retrying…', {
  retry: () => { /* re-queue this turn */ }
});
```

---

## **9. Existing Tests for SSE + Orchestrator**

**Orchestrator tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts`
- Helper: `buildMocks()` (registers mock services)
- Helper: `createTestHarness()` (spins up test bus + plugins)
- Pattern: mock `sandbox:open-session` to return a handle with resolved/rejected exited promise

**Transport tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/transport.test.ts`
- Mocks EventSource via fetch override
- Tests frame parsing + UIMessageChunk emission

**SSE tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/transport.test.ts` (same file)

---

## **10. Regression Test Plan**

**Where:** New test in `orchestrator.test.ts`

**Scenario:** "pod dies mid-turn (sandbox-exit-before-chat-end) → chat:turn-error fired"
1. Mock `sandbox:open-session` to return handle with instantly-resolved `.exited`
2. Mock `chat:turn-error` subscriber to capture emitted payloads
3. Call `agent:invoke`
4. Assert:
   - Outcome is `{ kind: 'terminated', reason: 'sandbox-exit-before-chat-end' }`
   - `chat:turn-error` was fired with the correct reqId + reason
   - `chat:end` was fired (idempotent audit-log invariant)
5. For SSE-specific test: spin up full SSE handler, verify error frame hits the stream

---

## **Summary: The Fix Blueprint**

| Component | Change | Priority |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| **orchestrator.ts** | Emit `chat:turn-error` on mid-turn sandbox exit (line ~1340 or ~1374) | **HIGH** |
| **types.ts** | Add `{ reqId, error }` variant to `SseFrame` union | HIGH |
| **sse.ts** | Wire `chat:turn-error` subscriber → safeWrite error frame + cleanup | HIGH |
| **plugin.ts** | Add `'chat:turn-error'` to subscribes manifest | HIGH |
| **transport.ts** | Parse error frame, emit finish chunk or metadata to runtime | HIGH |
| **agent-status-store** (runtime use) | Catch error finish, call `agentStatusActions.error()` with retry | MEDIUM |
| **orchestrator.test.ts** | Regression test: sandbox-exit-before-chat-end fires turn-error | MEDIUM |

---

**Most critical:** The orchestrator's `handle.exited` resolution (line 1338-1351) must fire a `chat:turn-error` event with the reqId so the SSE handler can emit an error frame before closing the stream. Without this, the client's flush() handler synthesizes a finish but never learns an error occurred.

Is there an event/promise that fires when the pod dies unexpectedly (vs a clean turn-end)? How does the orchestrator distinguish "runner exited cleanly after turn-end" from "runner died mid-turn"? Where are the logs `pod_exited` and `pod_killed` emitted, and with what context?

4. **The keepalive reaper** (PR #124 — `warmSessions`, `armReapTimer`, `handle.kill`, `idle-timeout`). When the host force-kills a wedged runner, what happens to any in-flight turn/SSE? When a turn is genuinely in-flight (not idle) and the pod dies externally, what code path runs?

5. **Client side** (`packages/channel-web/`). Find `AgentStatus` component and the SSE consumer (likely in `runtime.tsx` or a transport file). What SSE event types does the client handle? What makes the spinner show "Thinking…" vs flip to an error+retry state? What frame/event would the client need to receive to show error+retry? Is there a `.msg-error` / Toast / error surface it already supports? How does the client currently handle SSE close without a `done`? (Faults B/D in the same sweep show the client synthesizes a finish on a done-less close but surfaces nothing — find that code.)

6. **Existing tests** for the orchestrator SSE path and runner-exit handling — where do they live, what do they assert, what test helpers/mocks exist for the sandbox handle and SSE? I'll need to write a regression test.

7. The `agent:invoke` hook contract and the SSE-emitter hook (if streaming goes through a hook). What does the deferred/promise resolve to on a failed turn today — does `agent:invoke` reject, resolve with an error kind, or hang? Look for an outcome union like `{kind:'complete'|'cancelled'|'error'|...}`.

Report a concise structured map. I especially need: (a) the exact place a turn-error SSE frame should be emitted on mid-turn runner death, (b) the existing SSE frame format so a new error frame matches it, (c) what the client needs to receive, (d) where the regression test goes.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with the findings.

Based on my thorough search of the ax-next codebase, here is a structured map of the SSE streaming, chat orchestrator, sandbox handling, and relevant code paths for implementing the mid-turn runner death → error-frame fix:

## **1. The SSE Stream Endpoint (`/api/chat/stream/:reqId`)**

**Files:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts` (SSE handler + buffer mgmt)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/types.ts` (frame type definitions)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/server/routes-chat.ts` (route registration)

**Key Facts:**
- **Endpoint:** `GET /api/chat/stream/:reqId` (line 132, sse.ts)
- **Handler:** `createSseHandler()` (line 79-304, sse.ts) — wires 4 subscriptions:
  1. `chat:stream-chunk` → emits content deltas to client
  2. `chat:phase` → emits out-of-band status ("Starting sandbox…")
  3. `chat:turn-end` → emits done frame + cleanup
  4. Keepalive comment (`:[\n\n]` every 25s)
  
**Current Frame Format** (`SseFrame` union, types.ts:64-67):
```typescript
type SseFrame =
  | StreamChunk  // { reqId, kind: 'text'|'thinking'|'tool-use'|'tool-result', ...payload }
  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
  | { reqId: string; done: true }
```

**Wire Format:** Each frame is `data: ${JSON.stringify(frame)}\n\n` (sse.ts:86)

**Done-frame emission:** Line 275, sse.ts — fires on `chat:turn-end` subscriber match (by `ctx.conversationId`), then closes stream (line 279).

**Error-frame path TODAY:** None. No error variant exists in `SseFrame` union. Stream simply closes when `handle.exited` resolves.

---

## **2. Chat Orchestrator (Streaming Deltas → Outcome Resolution)**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`

**Flow:**
- **`runAgentInvoke()`** (line 584-1427):
  1. `chat:start` veto (line 589)
  2. `agents:resolve` ACL (line 615)
  3. J6 Routing decision (line 658-816): route to existing session or open fresh?
  4. Fresh-spawn path (line 818-1427):
     - `proxy:open-session` (line 1067)
     - Register waiter deferred (line 1145)
     - `sandbox:open-session` (line 1186) — returns `handle` with `.exited` promise
     - Enqueue user message (line 1284)
     - **Await chat:end OR sandbox exit OR timeout** (line 1338-1365)
     - Return `AgentOutcome` (line 1355)
  5. Routed path (line 705-816): same flow but reuses existing sandbox, no handle ownership

**Sandbox Exit Handling** (line 1338-1351):
```typescript
handle.exited
  .then(() => {
    if (!deferred.settled) {
      resolvedByChatEndSubscriber = false;
      deferred.resolve({
        kind: 'terminated',
        reason: 'sandbox-exit-before-chat-end',
      });
    }
  })
  .catch(() => {/* swallow */});
```

**Critical Gap:** When `handle.exited` resolves mid-turn (pod dies, no chat:end fire), orchestrator resolves its deferred with `terminated` outcome but **DOES NOT fire any SSE error frame**. The SSE stream just hangs waiting for `chat:turn-end`.

**Waiters tracking:** Map keyed by `ctx.reqId` (not sessionId) per line 473. When orchestrator synthesizes a `terminated` outcome and fires `chat:end` (line 1374), the event is broadcast but no frame reaches the SSE stream.

---

## **3. Sandbox Handle / K8s Provider (Pod Exit Detection)**

**Files:** 
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/sandbox-k8s/src/lifecycle.ts` (watchPodExit)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/sandbox-k8s/src/open-session.ts` (pod lifecycle + handle creation)

**Handle Structure** (open-session.ts:155-158):
```typescript
interface OpenSessionHandle {
  kill(): Promise<void>;
  exited: Promise<ExitInfo>;  // { code, signal, reason }
}
```

**Exit Detection** (`watchPodExit`, lifecycle.ts:162-221):
- Polls `readNamespacedPod()` every 250ms until phase reaches terminal state
- Returns `ExitInfo` with code/signal/reason (line 140-151)
- Distinguishes: `pod-gone`, `OOMKilled`, `Error`, `Evicted`, `DeadlineExceeded`, etc. (line 200)
- **Logs:** `pod_exited` event at line 209 with `{ phase, code, reason, containerReason, podReason }`

**Log Entry Example** (from bug description): `pod_exited code:143 + pod_killed`
- Code 143 = SIGTERM (128 + 15)
- Reason would be `pod_killed` if k8s deleted it externally

---

## **4. How Streamed Deltas Reach SSE**

**Path:**
1. Runner emits `event.stream-chunk` POST to `/ipc/event/stream-chunk` (IPC server listener in presets)
2. IPC server fires `chat:stream-chunk` hook event with `StreamChunk` payload
3. Channel-web's buffer-fill subscriber captures it (plugin.ts:97-101)
4. **Per-connection** SSE subscriber listens and emits (sse.ts:240-250):
   ```typescript
   deps.bus.subscribe<StreamChunk>('chat:stream-chunk', chunkSubKey, async (_ctx, payload) => {
     if (payload.reqId !== reqId) return undefined;  // filter by reqId
     safeWrite(payload);
     return undefined;
   });
   ```
5. `safeWrite()` JSON-encodes frame and writes `data: ...\n\n` to stream

**ReqId as join key:** Server-minted at POST time (routes-chat.ts:402), stamped onto every chunk by runner, matched by SSE handler.

---

## **5. Client-Side SSE Consumption**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts`

**Parser:** `processResponseStream()` (line 356-544)
- Splits on `\n\n`, parses each `data: {...}` JSON line
- **Emission policy:**
  - `{ reqId, text, kind: 'text'|'thinking' }` → emits `text-delta` UIMessageChunk
  - `{ reqId, phase }` → side-channel: `agentStatusActions.set(label)` (line 451-452)
  - `{ reqId, done: true }` → `closeOpen()` + emit `finish` chunk + set `finished=true` (line 441-445)
  - **Stream close without done frame** (line 533-540): `flush()` synthesizes `finish` — **client sees finish but NO error signal**

**UI Layer** (`AgentStatus.tsx`, lines 34-122):
- Modes: `'hidden'` (collapsed), `'working'` (blue dot, breathing), `'error'` (red dot, persistent)
- Error mode shows retry button (via `agentStatusActions.error(text, { retry, dismiss })`)
- **Gap:** No code path calls `.error()` on SSE stream failure; only side-effect is synthetic finish

**Agent-Status Store** (`agent-status-store.ts`):
- `agentStatusActions.error(text, opts)` sets mode='error' + text + retry/dismiss callbacks (line 106-117)
- When user clicks retry, custom handlers (registered by runtime) re-queue the turn

---

## **6. Current Test Coverage**

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts`

**Relevant Tests:**
- Line 364-406: "sandbox exits before chat:end" — orchestrator resolves `terminated` outcome, fires `chat:end`
- Line 408-456: Parametrized sweep: start-reject, queue-fail, open-fail, sandbox-exit scenarios
- **Gap:** No test for SSE stream still open when sandbox exits; no test for error-frame emission

---

## **7. Key Data Shapes & Flow**

**Agent:invoke → agent:invoke outcome:**
```typescript
type AgentOutcome = 
  | { kind: 'complete'; messages: UIMessage[] }
  | { kind: 'terminated'; reason: string; error?: unknown }
```
(Routes through `chat:end` event, keyed by sessionId for resolution.)

**ChannelWeb route dispatch** (routes-chat.ts:507-518):
```typescript
void bus.call<AgentInvokeInput, unknown>('agent:invoke', agentInvokeCtx, { message })
  .catch((err) => { /* log, don't await */ });
res.status(202).json({ conversationId, reqId });  // early return
```
Fire-and-forget; SSE stream already open on client side.

---

## **8. CRITICAL CODE LOCATIONS FOR THE FIX**

### **(a) Where to emit the error frame:**

**`orchestrator.ts` line 1338-1351** (`handle.exited` resolution):
Currently resolves deferred with `terminated` outcome. **NEW CODE:** also emit an error SSE frame via `bus.fire('chat:turn-error', ctx, { reqId, reason })` or similar.

**OR (better):**
**`orchestrator.ts` line 1373-1375** (failure path that synthesizes chat:end):
When `!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber`, fire both `chat:turn-error` AND `chat:end`.

### **(b) Hook to emit error frame on SSE:**

**`channel-web/src/server/plugin.ts` line 80 (subscribes array):**
Add `'chat:turn-error'` to the subscribes list.

**`channel-web/src/server/sse.ts` (new code):**
Add a subscriber for `chat:turn-error` (like the existing turn-end one, lines 264-285):
```typescript
deps.bus.subscribe<{ reqId?: string; reason?: string }>(
  'chat:turn-error',
  turnErrorSubKey,
  async (ctx, payload) => {
    if (payload.reqId !== reqId) return undefined;
    safeWrite({ reqId, error: payload.reason ?? 'unknown' });
    deps.buffer.evictReqId(reqId);
    cleanup();
    try { stream.close(); } catch { }
    return undefined;
  },
);
```

### **(c) New SseFrame variant:**

**`channel-web/src/server/types.ts` line 64-67:**
Add error variant:
```typescript
export type SseFrame =
  | StreamChunk
  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
  | { reqId: string; done: true }
  | { reqId: string; error: string };  // NEW
```

### **(d) Client-side error handler:**

**`channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts` line 440-479:**
In the frame-parsing loop, add:
```typescript
if ('error' in frame && typeof frame.error === 'string') {
  closeOpen(controller);
  // Emit an error chunk so the runtime can show error UI.
  // The AI SDK doesn't have a native error-chunk type, so we
  // emit a tool-output-error or use metadata to signal error.
  controller.enqueue({
    type: 'finish',
    finishReason: 'error',  // if AI SDK supports it
    // else: use metadata piggyback or a custom chunk type
  });
  finished = true;
  return;
}
```

**AND in the runtime hook** (that watches the stream chunks):
When a finish with `finishReason === 'error'` arrives, call:
```typescript
agentStatusActions.error('Runner died unexpectedly — retrying…', {
  retry: () => { /* re-queue this turn */ }
});
```

---

## **9. Existing Tests for SSE + Orchestrator**

**Orchestrator tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts`
- Helper: `buildMocks()` (registers mock services)
- Helper: `createTestHarness()` (spins up test bus + plugins)
- Pattern: mock `sandbox:open-session` to return a handle with resolved/rejected exited promise

**Transport tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/transport.test.ts`
- Mocks EventSource via fetch override
- Tests frame parsing + UIMessageChunk emission

**SSE tests:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/transport.test.ts` (same file)

---

## **10. Regression Test Plan**

**Where:** New test in `orchestrator.test.ts`

**Scenario:** "pod dies mid-turn (sandbox-exit-before-chat-end) → chat:turn-error fired"
1. Mock `sandbox:open-session` to return handle with instantly-resolved `.exited`
2. Mock `chat:turn-error` subscriber to capture emitted payloads
3. Call `agent:invoke`
4. Assert:
   - Outcome is `{ kind: 'terminated', reason: 'sandbox-exit-before-chat-end' }`
   - `chat:turn-error` was fired with the correct reqId + reason
   - `chat:end` was fired (idempotent audit-log invariant)
5. For SSE-specific test: spin up full SSE handler, verify error frame hits the stream

---

## **Summary: The Fix Blueprint**

| Component | Change | Priority |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| **orchestrator.ts** | Emit `chat:turn-error` on mid-turn sandbox exit (line ~1340 or ~1374) | **HIGH** |
| **types.ts** | Add `{ reqId, error }` variant to `SseFrame` union | HIGH |
| **sse.ts** | Wire `chat:turn-error` subscriber → safeWrite error frame + cleanup | HIGH |
| **plugin.ts** | Add `'chat:turn-error'` to subscribes manifest | HIGH |
| **transport.ts** | Parse error frame, emit finish chunk or metadata to runtime | HIGH |
| **agent-status-store** (runtime use) | Catch error finish, call `agentStatusActions.error()` with retry | MEDIUM |
| **orchestrator.test.ts** | Regression test: sandbox-exit-before-chat-end fires turn-error | MEDIUM |

---

**Most critical:** The orchestrator's `handle.exited` resolution (line 1338-1351) must fire a `chat:turn-error` event with the reqId so the SSE handler can emit an error frame before closing the stream. Without this, the client's flush() handler synthesizes a finish but never learns an error occurred.

1	import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
2	import { z } from 'zod';
3	import {
4	  PluginError,
5	  type AgentContext,
6	  type HookBus,
7	  type Logger,
8	} from '@ax/core';
9	import type { ResolvedSandboxK8sConfig } from './config.js';
10	import type { K8sCoreApi } from './k8s-api.js';
11	import { killPod } from './kill.js';
12	import { watchPodExit, waitForPodReady, type ExitInfo } from './lifecycle.js';
13	import { buildPodSpec } from './pod-spec.js';
14	
15	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	// sandbox:open-session — k8s impl.
17	//
18	// Mirrors the @ax/sandbox-subprocess shape:
19	//   1. Validate input.
20	//   2. Mint pod name + per-pod child logger (reqId, podName, synthetic pid).
21	//   3. Call session:create to mint sessionId + token.
22	//   4. Build pod spec with sessionId/token/requestId/runnerBinary in env
23	//      plus AX_RUNNER_ENDPOINT = config.hostIpcUrl (the cluster Service
24	//      URL pointing at the host pod's @ax/ipc-http listener).
25	//   5. createNamespacedPod.
26	//   6. Wait for Ready (we still need the pod IP for liveness, just not
27	//      for runnerEndpoint construction).
28	//   7. Wire `exited` from a long-lived watchPodExit poll.
29	//   8. Cleanup-on-exit: terminate session + delete pod (idempotent).
30	//
31	// Failures roll back: any exception after step 3 calls session:terminate
32	// before re-throwing, so a partial init doesn't leave a token alive with
33	// no pod.
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	const PLUGIN_NAME = '@ax/sandbox-k8s';
37	const HOOK_NAME = 'sandbox:open-session';
38	
39	// Owner triple — same shape sandbox-subprocess accepts. Forwarded to
40	// session:create so the v2 row is written atomically with v1.
41	export const AgentConfigSchema = z.object({
42	  systemPrompt: z.string(),
43	  allowedTools: z.array(z.string()),
44	  mcpConfigIds: z.array(z.string()),
45	  model: z.string(),
46	});
47	
48	// ProxyConfig — see chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts for the
49	// authoritative shape. Re-declared structurally here so this file stays
50	// free of cross-package imports (Invariant I2). Both `endpoint` and
51	// `unixSocketPath` are optional + mutually exclusive at construction
52	// time (the orchestrator's `endpointToProxyConfig` enforces it). For
53	// k8s today only `unixSocketPath` is meaningful — pod-spec mounts the
54	// socket into the runner via hostPath. `endpoint` (TCP) is accepted
55	// for forward-compat but currently unused on this side.
56	export const ProxyConfigSchema = z.object({
57	  endpoint: z.string().optional(),
58	  unixSocketPath: z.string().optional(),
59	  caCertPem: z.string().min(1),
60	  envMap: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),

1	import type { AgentOutcome, Plugin } from '@ax/core';
2	import {
3	  createOrchestrator,
4	  PLUGIN_NAME,
5	  type ChatOrchestratorConfig,
6	  type AgentInvokeInput,
7	} from './orchestrator.js';
8	
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	// @ax/chat-orchestrator plugin
11	//
12	// Registers `agent:invoke` — the host-side entrypoint a CLI (or any other host)
13	// uses to drive a single user→agent turn-sequence. Subscribes to `chat:end`
14	// so the orchestrator can capture the outcome the runner emits via its
15	// /event.chat-end IPC POST.
16	//
17	// Everything spicy happens inside `createOrchestrator`; this file is just
18	// the manifest + hook registration.
19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	export function createChatOrchestratorPlugin(
22	  config: ChatOrchestratorConfig,
23	): Plugin {
24	  return {
25	    manifest: {
26	      name: PLUGIN_NAME,
27	      version: '0.0.0',
28	      registers: ['agent:invoke'],
29	      // The orchestrator drives the per-chat lifecycle by calling these
30	      // peers. `session:create` is NOT listed — sandbox:open-session mints
31	      // the session itself; a double-create throws duplicate-session. The
32	      // orchestrator intentionally delegates session minting to the sandbox
33	      // plugin. `ipc:stop` is NOT listed either: sandbox-subprocess stops
34	      // its own per-session listener on child-close (no host-side caller
35	      // needed), and the k8s preset uses @ax/ipc-http which has a
36	      // process-scoped listener with no per-session start/stop hooks.
37	      //
38	      // `agents:resolve` is a HARD dep as of Week 9.5 — every chat goes
39	      // through the ACL gate before the sandbox is spawned. This forces
40	      // any preset that wires the orchestrator to also wire @ax/agents,
41	      // which is the intended coupling.
42	      calls: [
43	        'session:queue-work',
44	        'session:terminate',
45	        'sandbox:open-session',
46	        'agents:resolve',
47	      ],
48	      // ----- conditionally-called peers (NOT in `calls`) -----
49	      //
50	      // Week 10–12 Task 16 (J6) introduces `conversations:get`,
51	      // `conversations:bind-session`, and `session:is-alive`. The
52	      // orchestrator dispatches these ONLY when `ctx.conversationId` is
53	      // set — i.e. on the channel-web path that ALSO loads
54	      // @ax/conversations + a session backend with the new hook. Plugins
55	      // outside that path (CLI canary, mcp-client e2e harness) drive the
56	      // orchestrator without a conversation context, so they don't need
57	      // the peers loaded.
58	      //
59	      // Phase 2B adds `system-prompt:augment` to this same category. The
60	      // hook is registered by @ax/memory-strata (auto-inject path) and any
61	      // future personalization / tenant-policy provider. Listing it in
62	      // `calls` would force every preset wiring the orchestrator to also
63	      // wire a provider — but the CLI canary, the single-tenant preset,
64	      // and any deploy that doesn't load memory-strata MUST stay functional
65	      // without one. The orchestrator gates with `bus.hasService(...)` and
66	      // no-ops when absent.
67	      //
68	      // We gate each call with `bus.hasService(...)` at runtime to keep
69	      // the orchestrator usable in those non-channel-web presets. Failing
70	      // closed (no routing, no bind) is the correct degraded behavior —
71	      // a missing conversations plugin can't keep state about active
72	      // sessions anyway.
73	      //
74	      // Same pattern as the existing `ipc:stop` non-declaration: when a
75	      // hook is conditionally consumed, we don't declare it.
76	      // We listen to `chat:end` to capture the outcome emitted by the
77	      // runner (via the IPC server's /event.chat-end handler). The
78	      // subscriber is a pass-through: resolves the waiting deferred,
79	      // returns undefined, doesn't veto and doesn't transform.
80	      //
81	      // We also listen to `chat:turn-end` so that in one-shot mode
82	      // (the default for 6.5a) we queue a `cancel` into the runner's
83	      // inbox after the first user message completes — the runner is
84	      // persistent by design, so without this signal it would block
85	      // forever on inbox.next() and the agent:invoke would time out.
86	      subscribes: ['chat:end', 'chat:turn-end'],
87	    },
88	    init({ bus }) {
89	      const orch = createOrchestrator(bus, config);
90	
91	      bus.registerService<AgentInvokeInput, AgentOutcome>(
92	        'agent:invoke',
93	        PLUGIN_NAME,
94	        async (ctx, input) => orch.runAgentInvoke(ctx, input),
95	      );
96	
97	      bus.subscribe<{ outcome: AgentOutcome }>(
98	        'chat:end',
99	        PLUGIN_NAME,
100	        async (ctx, payload) => {
101	          orch.onChatEnd(ctx, payload);
102	          return undefined; // pass-through; no transform, no veto.
103	        },
104	      );
105	
106	      bus.subscribe<{ reason?: string; reqId?: string }>(
107	        'chat:turn-end',
108	        PLUGIN_NAME,
109	        async (ctx, payload) => {
110	          orch.onTurnEnd(ctx, payload);
111	          return undefined;
112	        },
113	      );
114	    },
115	  };
116	}

1	import {
2	  makeAgentContext,
3	  type AgentContext,
4	  type HookBus,
5	  type Plugin,
6	} from '@ax/core';
7	import { createChunkBuffer, type ChunkBuffer } from './chunk-buffer.js';
8	import { registerAttachmentsRoutes } from './routes-attachments.js';
9	import { registerChatRoutes } from './routes-chat.js';
10	import {
11	  createBufferFillSubscriber,
12	  createPhaseFillSubscriber,
13	  createSseHandler,
14	  createTurnEndEvictor,
15	  type RouteRequest,
16	  type RouteResponse,
17	} from './sse.js';
18	import { createTitleEventsHandler } from './title-events.js';
19	import type { PhaseEvent, StreamChunk } from './types.js';
20	
21	const PLUGIN_NAME = '@ax/channel-web';
22	
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// @ax/channel-web host-side plugin shell.
25	//
26	// Today: registers GET /api/chat/stream/:reqId for browser SSE consumers
27	// (Task 7 of Week 10–12). Tasks 9–13 extend this same plugin with the
28	// rest of the /api/chat/* surface (POST messages, GET conversations, etc.)
29	// — those slices land here so channel-web's wire surface stays in one
30	// place.
31	//
32	// Single-replica only (J8). Multi-replica fan-out is a Week 13+ slice
33	// that swaps the in-process chunk buffer for a redis stream / pg
34	// logical replication path; the SSE handler stays the same.
35	//
36	// Manifest:
37	//   - registers: nothing (the API surface is HTTP routes, not bus hooks).
38	//   - calls: http:register-route, auth:require-user, agents:resolve,
39	//     agents:list-for-user, conversations:get-by-req-id,
40	//     conversations:create / :get / :list / :delete,
41	//     agent:invoke. All hard — the chat-flow surface can't function without
42	//     any of them.
43	//   - subscribes: chat:stream-chunk (fills the buffer + per-connection
44	//     filter), chat:phase (single-slot phase memory + per-connection
45	//     filter), chat:turn-end (host-side eviction so the buffer doesn't
46	//     grow unbounded for streams nobody listens to).
47	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type
50	export interface ChannelWebServerConfig {}
51	
52	export function createChannelWebServerPlugin(
53	  _config: ChannelWebServerConfig = {},
54	): Plugin {
55	  let buffer: ChunkBuffer | undefined;
56	  const unregisterRoutes: Array<() => void> = [];
57	  let busRef: HookBus | undefined;
58	
59	  return {
60	    manifest: {
61	      name: PLUGIN_NAME,
62	      version: '0.0.0',
63	      registers: [],
64	      calls: [
65	        'http:register-route',
66	        'auth:require-user',
67	        'agents:resolve',
68	        'agents:list-for-user',
69	        'conversations:get-by-req-id',
70	        'conversations:create',
71	        'conversations:get',
72	        'conversations:list',
73	        'conversations:delete',
74	        'agent:invoke',
75	        // Phase 3 — attachments & artifacts.
76	        'attachments:store-temp',
77	        'attachments:commit',
78	        'attachments:download',
79	      ],
80	      subscribes: ['chat:stream-chunk', 'chat:phase', 'chat:turn-end', 'conversations:title-updated'],
81	    },
82	
83	    async init({ bus }) {
84	      busRef = bus;
85	      const initCtx: AgentContext = makeAgentContext({
86	        sessionId: 'init',
87	        agentId: PLUGIN_NAME,
88	        userId: 'system',
89	      });
90	
91	      const localBuffer = createChunkBuffer();
92	      buffer = localBuffer;
93	
94	      // Buffer-fill subscriber — host-side, captures every chunk
95	      // regardless of whether anyone's listening yet. Without this,
96	      // the SSE handler's `tail()` replay would always be empty.
97	      bus.subscribe<StreamChunk>(
98	        'chat:stream-chunk',
99	        `${PLUGIN_NAME}/buffer-fill`,
100	        createBufferFillSubscriber(localBuffer),
101	      );
102	
103	      // Phase-fill subscriber — same role as buffer-fill but for the
104	      // single-slot phase memory. Without this, an SSE consumer that
105	      // attaches after sandbox-k8s fires `chat:phase` would never see
106	      // the "Starting sandbox…" label.
107	      bus.subscribe<PhaseEvent>(
108	        'chat:phase',
109	        `${PLUGIN_NAME}/phase-fill`,
110	        createPhaseFillSubscriber(localBuffer),
111	      );
112	
113	      // Turn-end evictor — pre-cleans the buffer for terminated turns
114	      // so the per-connection turn-end subscriber doesn't have to be
115	      // present for the buffer to drain.
116	      bus.subscribe<{ reqId?: string }>(
117	        'chat:turn-end',
118	        `${PLUGIN_NAME}/turn-end-evictor`,
119	        createTurnEndEvictor(localBuffer),
120	      );
121	
122	      const handler = createSseHandler({
123	        bus,
124	        initCtx,
125	        buffer: localBuffer,
126	      });
127	
128	      const routeResult = await bus.call<
129	        unknown,
130	        { unregister: () => void }
131	      >('http:register-route', initCtx, {
132	        method: 'GET',
133	        path: '/api/chat/stream/:reqId',
134	        // The http-server's HttpRequest/HttpResponse are the canonical
135	        // shapes; our duck-typed RouteRequest/RouteResponse are a
136	        // structural subset (Invariant I2 — no @ax/http-server import in
137	        // sse.ts). Cast through `unknown` because exactOptionalProperty-
138	        // Types treats the structural intersection differently than the
139	        // `HttpRouteHandler` parameter declaration.
140	        handler: handler as unknown as (
141	          req: RouteRequest,
142	          res: RouteResponse,
143	        ) => Promise<void>,
144	      });
145	      unregisterRoutes.push(routeResult.unregister);
146	
147	      // Live title push — per-user SSE. Surfaces a title that lands after
148	      // the client's poll window without a reload (design I5: ships with
149	      // its consumer in the same PR; the route auto-registers here, no
150	      // preset change needed).
151	      const titleEventsHandler = createTitleEventsHandler({ bus, initCtx });
152	      const titleEventsRoute = await bus.call<
153	        unknown,
154	        { unregister: () => void }
155	      >('http:register-route', initCtx, {
156	        method: 'GET',
157	        path: '/api/chat/title-events',
158	        handler: titleEventsHandler as unknown as (
159	          req: RouteRequest,
160	          res: RouteResponse,
161	        ) => Promise<void>,
162	      });
163	      unregisterRoutes.push(titleEventsRoute.unregister);
164	
165	      // Tasks 9-13 — chat-flow REST surface (POST messages, GET/DELETE
166	      // conversations, GET conversations/:id, GET agents). CSRF gated
167	      // automatically by @ax/http-server's subscriber on state-changing
168	      // methods. See routes-chat.ts for per-endpoint details.
169	      const chatRouteUnregisters = await registerChatRoutes(bus, initCtx);
170	      for (const u of chatRouteUnregisters) unregisterRoutes.push(u);
171	
172	      // Phase 3 — attachments + downloads. Closes the half-wired window
173	      // opened in Phase 1 (routes-chat.ts already calls
174	      // `attachments:commit` for attachment_ref blocks); declaring the
175	      // three hooks in `manifest.calls` + registering the two routes here
176	      // makes the surface complete.
177	      const attachmentRouteUnregisters = await registerAttachmentsRoutes(
178	        bus,
179	        initCtx,
180	      );
181	      for (const u of attachmentRouteUnregisters) unregisterRoutes.push(u);
182	    },
183	
184	    async shutdown() {
185	      // Drop the route first so a re-init doesn't hit duplicate-route.
186	      while (unregisterRoutes.length > 0) {
187	        const fn = unregisterRoutes.pop();
188	        try {
189	          fn?.();
190	        } catch {
191	          // best-effort
192	        }
193	      }
194	      // Drop our subscribers so a re-init in tests doesn't leave the
195	      // old buffer-fill closure ALONGSIDE the new one.
196	      busRef?.unsubscribe('chat:stream-chunk', `${PLUGIN_NAME}/buffer-fill`);
197	      busRef?.unsubscribe('chat:phase', `${PLUGIN_NAME}/phase-fill`);
198	      busRef?.unsubscribe(
199	        'chat:turn-end',
200	        `${PLUGIN_NAME}/turn-end-evictor`,
201	      );
202	      busRef = undefined;
203	      // Stop the chunk buffer's sweep timer.
204	      buffer?.dispose();
205	      buffer = undefined;
206	    },
207	  };
208	}
209

Main checkout's decisions.md is the canonical gitignored, branch-independent log (worktree's is empty). | Work in-place (rejected: concurrent branch-switchers); decisions in worktree (rejected: fragments memory) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fix = add `cause: { actualParent: currentVersion }` to the **`workspace:apply-internal` parent-CAS throw** (impl.ts:791-798), the FileChange sibling of F-1's apply-bundle Site 1 (decision 41) | The consumer `attachments:commit` starts `parent:null` and rebases ONLY on `'actualParent' in err.cause`; the core backend's apply-internal threw it bare → re-throw → 500 on the single-replica backend. | 2026-05-23 | Fault-A yolo-ship: isolated worktree `.claude/worktrees/fault-a-sse-turn-error` off origin/main; plan doc copied in (uncommitted); decisions.md written to **main checkout** | Same as decisions 38/40 — shared checkout + concurrent agents ([[feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree]]). | Work in-place (rejected) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fault A fix mechanism = new broadcast event **`chat:turn-error` {reqId, reason}** fired by the orchestrator, consumed by channel-web's per-connection SSE subscriber (match by reqId) → emits a new `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}` + closes the stream | The SSE handler ONLY listens to `chat:turn-end` (done frame); on a *terminated* outcome the orchestrator fires `chat:end` (audit) but NEVER `chat:turn-end`, so the stream hangs forever (keepalive 25s heartbeat keeps it open). A dedicated turn-error event is cleaner+safer than synthesizing a `chat:turn-end` (which would trip keepalive's `onTurnEnd` reap-arm, conversation-titles, audit-log — all of which expect a *real completed* turn). Reuses the existing per-connection-subscriber + reqId-match pattern. | Reuse `chat:turn-end` w/ an error flag (rejected: pollutes ~5 turn-end subscribers that assume completion); subscribe SSE to `chat:end` + check `outcome.kind!=='complete'` (rejected: the routed path's `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` flag stays true when a waiter resolves out-of-band, so chat:end would be missed — fragile coupling) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fire `chat:turn-error` at TWO complementary orchestrator points: (a) the synthesized-terminated chokepoints `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` in BOTH fresh-spawn (~L1373) and routed (~L811) paths — covers sandbox-exit + wedged-runner **timeout** + chat-run-error; (b) a new **`session:terminate` subscriber** that fires turn-error for in-flight unsettled reqIds on the dying session — covers the routed/warm path **promptly** (else it waits the full 10-min `chatTimeoutMs`) | Two death modes: pod gone (→ `session:terminate` fires, fast on both paths) vs runner process wedged but pod alive (→ no session:terminate, only the bounded timeout). (a) covers the wedge; (b) covers pod-death promptly on the keepalive-default routed path. `session:terminate` is a fired event `{sessionId}` (session store re-broadcasts after the service work — session-postgres/plugin.ts:623) so the orchestrator can subscribe. Guard (b) with `!deferred.settled` to skip completed turns; SSE-already-closed makes any spurious fire a harmless no-op anyway. | Only fresh-spawn handle.exited (rejected: leaves warm-reuse turns hanging ~10min — keepalive is default in k8s); resolve/reject the routed deferred from session:terminate (rejected: resolving keeps `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` true → chat:end missed; rejecting needs a new error class + 2 catch edits — the lingering-until-timeout waiter is already the documented routed behavior at orchestrator.ts:785-789, we only add a *prompt SSE error* on top) |
| 2026-05-23 | Client: transport emits the AI-SDK-native **`{type:'error', errorText}`** UIMessageChunk on the error frame (terminates turn → `running` flips false); runtime `useChat({onError})` drives **`agentStatusActions.error(msg,{retry: ()=>regenerate()})`** via a chat ref. `agentStatusActions.error` already exists but was wired to NOTHING in prod (only tests + dev triggers) | `RunningEffect` unmount only `hide()`s when mode==='working', so setting error mode leaves the row persistent (red dot + retry) after the turn ends — exactly the desired "error+retry". `regenerate()` re-POSTs the last user turn (history is server-side; active_session_id was cleared by session:terminate's conversations subscriber → routes to a fresh sandbox). Reason→message map (ERROR_LABELS, default "The agent stopped unexpectedly. Retry to continue.") lives client-side, mirroring PHASE_LABELS. | Transport-direct `agentStatusActions.error` (rejected: retry=regenerate is per-thread, transport is shared across threads — can't reach regenerate); dismiss-only/no-retry (rejected: TODO explicitly says "error+retry") |
| 2026-05-23 | Scope: fix covers sandbox-death + wedged-runner-timeout (Fault A + the closely-related hang). Faults **B/D** (host bounce / network drop → silent finalize) stay a SEPARATE TODO item — NOT folded in | Reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention; B/D are a distinct GLITCH class (client synthesizes a done-less-close finish but surfaces nothing — a transport `flush()` concern, not an orchestrator-terminated-outcome concern). The chat-qa-sweep findings already track B/D separately. | Fold B/D in (rejected: scope creep, different root cause + fix site) |
| 2026-05-23 | security-checklist skill NOT formally invoked; brief security note in PR body instead | No sandbox boundary / IPC transport / plugin-loading / new-dep change, and `reason` is **host-orchestrator-generated** (a fixed code set), not model/tool/user output — not untrusted content per invariant #5. The new SSE `error` frame is rendered as escaped React text (agentStatusActions.error → text node), no XSS. | Full security-checklist (rejected: none of its triggers fire; the wire field is trusted host text) |
| 2026-05-23 | Implement via per-task subagents (subagent-driven-development), TDD per task | yolo-ship default; the change spans 3 packages (chat-orchestrator, channel-web server + client) with independent test surfaces — clean task boundaries. Orchestrator already holds the full map, so subagent prompts can be precise. | Inline (rejected here: multi-package, multiple test files; subagents keep the controller context lean for the ship loop) |
