41	| 2026-05-23 | F-1 (workspace-git-core): add `cause.actualParent` to the apply-bundle **parent-CAS throw (Site 1) ONLY**, not the post-CAS integrity sites (2: baseline mismatch, 3: ancestry) | Site 1 (`input.parent !== currentVersion`) is the genuine concurrent-writer detector — `currentVersion` is the freshly-read head that advanced past the runner's pinned parent. At Sites 2/3 `head === input.parent` already holds (no concurrent advance); they're real bad-bundle/baseline vetoes. Forwarding actualParent there would make the runner resync-retry against the same head (useless churn) then roll back anyway. | Set actualParent on all 3 sites to mirror the server backend's helper (rejected: server sites are genuine head-state cases; core Sites 2/3 are not — would trigger pointless resync loops) |
42	| 2026-05-23 | F-1 handler: apply-bundle catch fetches `baselineBundleBytes` AT `actualParent` via the **`workspace:export-baseline-bundle` hook** (not a direct backend import); on a double-race export parent-mismatch, forward the fresher envelope, else fall back to a bare veto; only forward the actualParent+bundle **pair** | Hook keeps invariant #2 (no cross-plugin import). Single-replica core's parent-mismatch carries only actualParent (no bundle), and core's export bundles AT the requested OID (no strict-head check) so `export({version:actualParent})` succeeds at the current head. The runner ignores actualParent without a bundle (loop guard requires both), so a partial envelope is pointless → emit the pair or a bare veto. Shared `resyncEnvelopeFromCause` helper de-dups the export + apply-bundle catches. | Import the backend to bundle directly (rejected: invariant #2); forward actualParent alone (rejected: runner can't act on it); re-throw to 500 on export double-race (rejected: reintroduces the original stuck-loop bug) |
43	| 2026-05-23 | F-1 regression test = **real-backend integration test in `@ax/ipc-core`** (real workspace-git-core on a HookBus + real commit-notify handler), `@ax/workspace-git-core` added as an ipc-core **devDependency** | The apply-bundle catch is only reachable with REAL valid bundles flowing through the full bundler pipeline (verify/walk/scratch) — a stub probe with garbage bundles fails earlier (proven by the existing line-240 handler test). Tests may cross-plugin import (eslint `no-restricted-imports` OFF for `__tests__`); no dep cycle (workspace-git-core → @ax/core only); empty-baseline env is identical across backends so OIDs line up. Asserts the handler RESPONSE `{accepted:false, actualParent:V2, baselineBundleBytes:<real bundle>}` from materialize→turn-1→concurrent-apply→turn-2. | Two within-package unit tests (core: throw carries actualParent; ipc-core stub: forwards envelope) (rejected: stub can't reach the apply-bundle catch with real bundles — weaker proof than the handoff's required integration assertion) |
44	| 2026-05-23 | F-2 helper `commitNotifyWithResync` lives in a **NEW module `commit-notify-resync.ts`**, importing git ops from `./git-workspace.js` — NOT inside git-workspace.ts | `main.test.ts` mocks `../git-workspace.js`; an ESM **intra-module** call (helper in git-workspace.ts calling advanceBaseline/resyncBaselineAndReplay in the same file) bypasses the mock, but a **cross-module** import resolves through it. Separate module ⇒ the existing mocks intercept the helper's git calls, so the real helper runs against mocked git ops + a fake client. | Put it in git-workspace.ts (rejected: mocks wouldn't intercept its internal calls → tests hit real git, no repo); mock the helper itself in main.test.ts (rejected: defeats testing the real wiring) |
45	| 2026-05-23 | F-2 helper preserves behavior **exactly**: returns `input.parentVersion` (unchanged) on `kept`/`rolled-back`, `resp.version` on accept, `pv` on reb===null; keeps the `outcome` field though callers consume only `parentVersion`; final-site logs lose the "final" prefix | The original inline loop mutated the outer `parentVersion` ONLY on accept / reb===null; kept/rolled-back left it at the pre-loop value — the helper mirrors that. `outcome` is handoff-specified + asserted by the helper unit test (a tested return contract, not dead code per YAGNI). Unifying both sites onto one helper is the explicit F-2 goal, so the cosmetic "final" log-prefix loss is acceptable. | Advance parentVersion on kept (rejected: changes behavior — original accumulates against the old parent); drop `outcome` as YAGNI (rejected: handoff contract + test assertions); add a logLabel param to keep "final" (rejected: surface for a cosmetic log) |
46	| 2026-05-23 | F-1/F-2 implemented via **2 sequential subagents** (F-1 then F-2), each TDD, despite disjoint packages | Subagents share the worktree filesystem + git index; concurrent `git commit`/`pnpm` would race (decision 25 lesson). Subagents keep the heavy per-task file reads out of the orchestrator's context for the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop. F-1 and F-2 touch disjoint packages so no merge conflict, but sequential avoids index races. | Parallel subagents (rejected: shared-fs git-index race); inline (rejected: F-1 integration test needs heavy contract.test.ts/server-test reads — would bloat orchestrator context before the ship loop) |

| 2026-05-23 | F-2 helper `commitNotifyWithResync` lives in a **NEW module `commit-notify-resync.ts`**, importing git ops from `./git-workspace.js` — NOT inside git-workspace.ts | `main.test.ts` mocks `../git-workspace.js`; an ESM **intra-module** call (helper in git-workspace.ts calling advanceBaseline/resyncBaselineAndReplay in the same file) bypasses the mock, but a **cross-module** import resolves through it. Separate module ⇒ the existing mocks intercept the helper's git calls, so the real helper runs against mocked git ops + a fake client. | Put it in git-workspace.ts (rejected: mocks wouldn't intercept its internal calls → tests hit real git, no repo); mock the helper itself in main.test.ts (rejected: defeats testing the real wiring) |
| 2026-05-23 | F-2 helper preserves behavior **exactly**: returns `input.parentVersion` (unchanged) on `kept`/`rolled-back`, `resp.version` on accept, `pv` on reb===null; keeps the `outcome` field though callers consume only `parentVersion`; final-site logs lose the "final" prefix | The original inline loop mutated the outer `parentVersion` ONLY on accept / reb===null; kept/rolled-back left it at the pre-loop value — the helper mirrors that. `outcome` is handoff-specified + asserted by the helper unit test (a tested return contract, not dead code per YAGNI). Unifying both sites onto one helper is the explicit F-2 goal, so the cosmetic "final" log-prefix loss is acceptable. | Advance parentVersion on kept (rejected: changes behavior — original accumulates against the old parent); drop `outcome` as YAGNI (rejected: handoff contract + test assertions); add a logLabel param to keep "final" (rejected: surface for a cosmetic log) |
| 2026-05-23 | F-1/F-2 implemented via **2 sequential subagents** (F-1 then F-2), each TDD, despite disjoint packages | Subagents share the worktree filesystem + git index; concurrent `git commit`/`pnpm` would race (decision 25 lesson). Subagents keep the heavy per-task file reads out of the orchestrator's context for the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop. F-1 and F-2 touch disjoint packages so no merge conflict, but sequential avoids index races. | Parallel subagents (rejected: shared-fs git-index race); inline (rejected: F-1 integration test needs heavy contract.test.ts/server-test reads — would bloat orchestrator context before the ship loop) |
| 2026-05-23 | apply-FileChange `actualParent` (F-1 sibling) yolo-ship: isolated worktree off origin/main; decisions.md written to **main checkout** | Same as decision 40 — handoff mandated a worktree + [[feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree]]. Main checkout's decisions.md is the canonical gitignored, branch-independent log (worktree's is empty). | Work in-place (rejected: concurrent branch-switchers); decisions in worktree (rejected: fragments memory) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fix = add `cause: { actualParent: currentVersion }` to the **`workspace:apply-internal` parent-CAS throw** (impl.ts:791-798), the FileChange sibling of F-1's apply-bundle Site 1 (decision 41) | The consumer `attachments:commit` starts `parent:null` and rebases ONLY on `'actualParent' in err.cause`; the core backend's apply-internal threw it bare → re-throw → 500 on the single-replica backend. One-line analog of impl.ts:1039 + the server backend's `parentMismatch` helper (the cross-backend contract). **`currentVersion` may be `null` here** (unlike apply-bundle Site 1, guarded by `currentVersion !== null`) — that's the correct echo (empty-mirror case; the consumer's `next === parent` guard breaks the loop). Apply-bundle Sites 2/3 stay bare (decision 41 reasoning — head==parent there, not a concurrent advance). | Guard with `currentVersion !== null` like apply-bundle (rejected: apply-internal's CAS fires on null-head-but-non-null-parent too, and echoing null is the right signal); touch other parent-mismatch sites (rejected: only the apply-FileChange CAS has a rebase-on-actualParent consumer) |
| 2026-05-23 | Tests: (1) **non-negotiable RED-before unit** in `@ax/workspace-git-core` asserting the real backend's apply parent-mismatch carries `cause.actualParent` (non-null head + null-head inverse); (2) **real-backend integration** in `attachments/src/__tests__/commit.test.ts` wiring the REAL `@ax/workspace-git-core` backend (new test-only devDep) into `createCommitHandler` and proving it recovers | The existing commit.test.ts "rebases on parent-mismatch" test uses a MOCKED backend that already echoes actualParent — it encodes the contract the real core backend was violating. The new integration value is the REAL backend echoing through the full handler path (seed mirror out-of-band → head non-null → handler starts parent:null → mismatch → rebase → applies). Cross-plugin devDep is test-only (eslint off in `__tests__`), acyclic (core→@ax/core only), and matches the `@ax/conversations`/`@ax/ipc-core` devDep precedent. Postgres harness already in commit.test.ts (reused). | Only the unit test (rejected: handoff strongly prefers a real-handler integration; mocked retry test doesn't catch a real-backend contract drift); duplicate the retry loop in workspace-git-core's own dir (rejected: doesn't exercise the real attachments handler) |
| 2026-05-23 | Implement **inline, strict TDD** (RED→fix→GREEN), not per-task subagents | ~3-line fix + 2 focused tests; full context already held (impl.ts both CAS sites, the consumer loop, both test harnesses); context well under cap. Mirrors decisions 19 + 38. The ship loop is where lean context matters. | Per-task subagents per yolo-ship default (rejected: re-deriving known context costs more, risks divergence on a tiny fully-specified diff) |
| 2026-05-23 | Cluster ops (`helm upgrade`, image verify) run against the **main checkout's chart**, not the worktree's | `helm upgrade` from the worktree chart fails with `missing in charts/ directory: postgresql` — the vendored subchart tarball (`deploy/charts/ax-next/charts/postgresql-*.tgz`) is gitignored, so `git worktree add` doesn't copy it. The first failed upgrade was a no-op (backend stayed `local`) but the paired `kubectl rollout restart` still fired — verify the env var actually flipped, don't trust the rollout. Use `helm upgrade ax-next /Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/deploy/charts/ax-next ...` (absolute main-checkout path) which has the deps vendored. | `helm dependency build` in the worktree (rejected: needs network fetch of the pinned postgresql chart; main checkout already has it); run from worktree (rejected: missing vendored dep) |

| 2026-05-23 | Fault-A yolo-ship: isolated worktree `.claude/worktrees/fault-a-sse-turn-error` off origin/main; plan doc copied in (uncommitted); decisions.md written to **main checkout** | Same as decisions 38/40 — shared checkout + concurrent agents ([[feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree]]). | Work in-place (rejected) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fault A fix mechanism = new broadcast event **`chat:turn-error` {reqId, reason}** fired by the orchestrator, consumed by channel-web's per-connection SSE subscriber (match by reqId) → emits a new `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}` + closes the stream | The SSE handler ONLY listens to `chat:turn-end` (done frame); on a *terminated* outcome the orchestrator fires `chat:end` (audit) but NEVER `chat:turn-end`, so the stream hangs forever (keepalive 25s heartbeat keeps it open). A dedicated turn-error event is cleaner+safer than synthesizing a `chat:turn-end` (which would trip keepalive's `onTurnEnd` reap-arm, conversation-titles, audit-log — all expect a *real completed* turn). Reuses the existing per-connection-subscriber + reqId-match pattern. | Reuse `chat:turn-end` w/ error flag (rejected: pollutes ~5 turn-end subscribers that assume completion); SSE subscribe to `chat:end` + check `outcome.kind!=='complete'` (rejected: routed path's `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` stays true on out-of-band resolve → chat:end semantics fragile) |
| 2026-05-23 | Fire `chat:turn-error` at TWO orchestrator points: (a) the `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` chokepoints in BOTH fresh-spawn (~L1373) and routed (~L811) paths — covers sandbox-exit + wedged-runner **timeout** + chat-run-error; (b) a new **`session:terminate` subscriber** firing turn-error for in-flight unsettled reqIds — covers the routed/warm path **promptly** (else it waits the full 10-min `chatTimeoutMs`) | Two death modes: pod gone (→ `session:terminate` fires, fast on both paths) vs runner process wedged but pod alive (→ no session:terminate, only the bounded timeout). `session:terminate` is a fired event `{sessionId}` re-broadcast by the session store (session-postgres/plugin.ts:623) so the orchestrator can subscribe. Guard (b) `!deferred.settled` to skip completed turns; SSE-already-closed makes any spurious fire a harmless no-op. | Only fresh-spawn handle.exited (rejected: warm-reuse turns hang ~10min — keepalive is k8s default); resolve/reject the routed deferred from session:terminate (rejected: resolving keeps the flag true → chat:end missed; rejecting needs a new error class + 2 catch edits — lingering-until-timeout is already the documented routed behavior at orchestrator.ts:785-789; we only add a *prompt SSE error*) |
| 2026-05-23 | Client: transport emits AI-SDK-native **`{type:'error', errorText}`** UIMessageChunk on the error frame (terminates turn → `running` false); runtime `useChat({onError})` drives **`agentStatusActions.error(msg,{retry:()=>regenerate()})`** via a chat ref. `agentStatusActions.error` already existed but was wired to NOTHING in prod (only tests + dev triggers) | `RunningEffect` unmount only `hide()`s when mode==='working', so setting error mode leaves the row persistent (red dot + retry). `regenerate()` re-POSTs the last user turn (history is server-side; active_session_id cleared by session:terminate → routes fresh). Reason→message map (ERROR_LABELS, default "The agent stopped unexpectedly. Retry to continue.") lives client-side, mirroring PHASE_LABELS. | Transport-direct `agentStatusActions.error` (rejected: retry=regenerate is per-thread, transport is shared — can't reach regenerate); dismiss-only (rejected: TODO says "error+retry") |
| 2026-05-23 | Scope: fix covers sandbox-death + wedged-runner-timeout (Fault A + the related hang). Faults **B/D** (host bounce / network drop → silent finalize) stay a SEPARATE TODO item — NOT folded in | Reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention; B/D are a distinct GLITCH class (client synthesizes a done-less-close finish but surfaces nothing — a transport `flush()` concern, not an orchestrator-terminated-outcome concern). chat-qa-sweep already tracks B/D separately. | Fold B/D in (rejected: scope creep, different root cause + fix site) |
| 2026-05-23 | security-checklist skill NOT formally invoked; brief security note in PR body instead | No sandbox boundary / IPC transport / plugin-loading / new-dep change; `reason` is **host-orchestrator-generated** (fixed code set), not model/tool/user output — not untrusted content per invariant #5. New SSE `error` frame renders as escaped React text, no XSS. | Full security-checklist (rejected: no triggers fire; wire field is trusted host text) |

Let me log that and write the plan.

input.content:
# Fault A — sandbox death mid-turn → SSE turn-error frame (impl plan)

## Problem

Kill the serving runner pod mid-turn → host logs `pod_exited code:143` + `pod_killed`,
but the client SSE (`GET /api/chat/stream/:reqId`, HTTP 200) never gets an error/`done`
frame → the `AgentStatus` "Thinking…" spinner + Stop persist indefinitely (the 25 s SSE
keepalive heartbeat keeps the connection open forever). Recoverable only by manual Stop.

### Root cause

`packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts` only emits a terminal frame on `chat:turn-end`
(the `{done:true}` frame, matched by `ctx.conversationId`). On a **terminated** outcome the
orchestrator fires `chat:end` (audit) — NOT `chat:turn-end` — so the SSE never gets a
terminal frame. Two death modes feed this:

- **Pod gone** (`kubectl delete pod`): the sandbox provider's exit handler calls
  `session:terminate`, which the session store re-broadcasts as a fired `session:terminate`
  event `{sessionId}` (session-postgres/plugin.ts:623). On the **fresh-spawn** path
  `handle.exited` ALSO resolves → terminated `'sandbox-exit-before-chat-end'` → `chat:end`
  (orchestrator.ts:1338-1375). On the **routed/warm** path the orchestrator does NOT watch
  `exited` (orchestrator.ts:785-789) — it resolves only via the 10-min `chatTimeoutMs`.
- **Runner wedged but pod alive**: no `session:terminate`; resolves via the bounded timeout
  → terminated `'chat-run-timeout'` → `chat:end`.

In every case `chat:end` fires but the SSE is deaf to it → infinite spinner.

## Fix (one mechanism)

A new broadcast event **`chat:turn-error` `{reqId, reason}`** fired by the orchestrator on
terminated turns, consumed by a per-connection SSE subscriber (match by `reqId`) → emits a
new `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}` + closes the stream. Client transport translates the error
frame into the AI-SDK `{type:'error', errorText}` UIMessageChunk (turn ends → `running`
false); the runtime's `useChat({onError})` flips `agentStatusActions` to error + a retry
(`regenerate()`).

Fired at TWO complementary orchestrator points:
- (a) the `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` chokepoints in BOTH fresh-spawn (~L1373) and
  routed (~L811) paths — covers sandbox-exit + wedged-runner timeout + chat-run-error.
- (b) a new `session:terminate` subscriber firing turn-error for in-flight **unsettled**
  reqIds on the dying session — covers the routed/warm pod-death path **promptly** (else it
  waits the full 10-min timeout). Guard `!deferred.settled` to skip completed turns;
  SSE-already-closed makes any spurious fire a harmless no-op.

Scope: Fault A (sandbox death) + the closely-related wedged-runner timeout. Faults B/D (host
bounce / network drop → silent finalize) stay a separate TODO item (a transport `flush()`
concern, different root cause).

## Boundary review (new `chat:turn-error` hook — required by CLAUDE.md)

- **Alternate impl:** a CLI channel would subscribe to render a terminal error line; a
  metrics plugin could count failed turns. Genuine broadcast w/ multiple potential
  subscribers (mirrors `chat:phase` / `chat:turn-end`). Not premature.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** `reqId` (server-minted correlation id, already the
  join key for `chat:stream-chunk`/`chat:phase`; transport-neutral), `reason` (stable
  backend-agnostic code: `sandbox-exit-before-chat-end`, `chat-run-timeout`,
  `sandbox-terminated`, `chat-run-error` — orchestrator-abstraction vocabulary, NOT
  `pod_name`/exit-code/`sha`). None leak.
- **Subscriber risk:** no backend-specific field; `reason` is orchestrator vocabulary, safe
  across the k8s/subprocess alternate impls.
- **Wire surface:** `chat:turn-error` is host-internal (orchestrator→channel-web), NOT IPC —
  it does not cross the runner⇄host boundary. The browser-facing `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}`
  schema lives in channel-web's own `types.ts`.

## Security note (no full security-checklist — no trigger fires)

No sandbox/IPC/plugin-loading/dependency change. `reason` is host-orchestrator-generated
(fixed code set), not model/tool/user output → not untrusted content. The new SSE `error`
field renders as escaped React text (`agentStatusActions.error` → text node), no XSS.

## Tasks (inline, strict TDD)

### Task 1 — channel-web server: SSE error frame + subscriber

Files: `packages/channel-web/src/server/types.ts`, `sse.ts`, `plugin.ts`,
`src/__tests__/sse.test.ts` (or wherever the SSE handler is tested).

1. `types.ts`: add `| { reqId: string; error: string }` to `SseFrame`. Doc it as the
   "turn-error terminator" alongside the done frame.
2. `sse.ts` `createSseHandler`: add a per-connection `chat:turn-error` subscriber (key
   `${PLUGIN_NAME}/sse-turn-error/${subscriberSuffix}`), filtered by `payload.reqId === reqId`
   (NOT conversationId — the orchestrator fires with the original `ctx.reqId`). On match:
   `safeWrite({ reqId, error: payload.reason })` → `deps.buffer.evictReqId(reqId)` →
   `cleanup()` → `stream.close()`. Add its unsubscribe to `cleanup()`.
3. `plugin.ts`: add `'chat:turn-error'` to manifest `subscribes`; add a boot-time evictor
   subscriber for `chat:turn-error` reusing `createTurnEndEvictor(localBuffer)` (payload has
   `reqId`), key `${PLUGIN_NAME}/turn-error-evictor`; unsubscribe it in `shutdown()`.

Tests (RED first): turn-error for the connection's reqId → `data: {"reqId":...,"error":...}`
frame written + stream closed + buffer evicted; turn-error for a DIFFERENT reqId → ignored
(no write, stream stays open).

### Task 2 — chat-orchestrator: fire `chat:turn-error`

Files: `packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`, `plugin.ts`,
`src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts`.

1. `orchestrator.ts`: add helper `fireTurnError(ctx, reqId, reason)` → `await bus.fire(
   'chat:turn-error', ctx, { reqId, reason })` (await for deterministic ordering in tests;
   broadcast, observation-only).
2. Fresh-spawn `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` block (~L1373): `await fireTurnError(ctx,
   ctx.reqId, outcome.reason)` BEFORE `bus.fire('chat:end', ...)` (outcome is always
   terminated here — has `.reason`).
3. Routed `if (!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` block (~L811): same.
4. Add `onSessionTerminate(ctx, payload: { sessionId })`: look up `reqIdsBySession.get(
   payload.sessionId)`; for each reqId whose `waitersByReqId.get(reqId)` exists and is
   `!settled`, `void bus.fire('chat:turn-error', ctx, { reqId, reason: 'sandbox-terminated' })`.
   Return it from `createOrchestrator`.
5. `plugin.ts`: add `'session:terminate'` to manifest `subscribes`; wire
   `bus.subscribe('session:terminate', PLUGIN_NAME, ...) → orch.onSessionTerminate(...)`.

Tests (RED first): (a) fresh-spawn sandbox-exit → `chat:turn-error` fired w/ the reqId +
reason `sandbox-exit-before-chat-end`; (b) timeout → fired w/ `chat-run-timeout`; (c)
`session:terminate` while a routed waiter is in-flight → fired w/ that reqId +
`sandbox-terminated`; (d) `session:terminate` with NO in-flight waiter → NOT fired; (e)
completed turn (chat:turn-end resolves) → NO turn-error.

### Task 3 — channel-web client transport: parse error frame

Files: `packages/channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts`, `src/__tests__/transport.test.ts`.

1. Add `| { reqId: string; error: string }` to the transport-local `SseFrame` type.
2. Add `ERROR_LABELS: Record<string, string>` (e.g. `'chat-run-timeout' → "The agent timed
   out. Retry to continue."`) + `DEFAULT_TURN_ERROR = "The agent stopped unexpectedly. Retry
   to continue."`.
3. In `processResponseStream` transform, after the `done` check and before the phase/kind
   checks: `if ('error' in frame && typeof frame.error === 'string') { closeOpen(controller);
   controller.enqueue({ type: 'error', errorText: ERROR_LABELS[frame.error] ??
   DEFAULT_TURN_ERROR }); finished = true; return; }`.

Tests (RED first): error frame → emitted chunks include `{type:'error', errorText:<message>}`
and the stream terminates (drain completes, no `{type:'finish'}` after the error, no hang);
error after a partial text delta → text part closed (`text-end`) then the error chunk.

### Task 4 — channel-web client runtime: onError → error+retry

Files: `packages/channel-web/src/lib/runtime.tsx`, plus a tiny testable helper +
`src/__tests__/` test.

1. Extract a pure helper `applyTurnError(error: unknown, regenerate: () => void)` (new small
   module or exported from runtime) that calls `agentStatusActions.error(error instanceof
   Error && error.message ? error.message : DEFAULT_TURN_ERROR, { retry: regenerate })`.
2. `useChatThreadRuntime`: hold `chatRef`; pass `onError: (e) => applyTurnError(e, () => {
   void chatRef.current?.regenerate(); })` to `useChat`; assign `chatRef.current = chat`.

Tests (RED first, on the helper): `applyTurnError(new Error('boom'), spy)` sets
agentStatusActions to mode='error', text='boom', retry set; calling retry invokes the spy;
non-Error input falls back to the default message.

## Verification

- `pnpm build` + `pnpm test --filter @ax/chat-orchestrator --filter @ax/channel-web` + lint
  (scope eslint to changed files per [[feedback_workspace_lint_stale_worktree_noise]]).
- Whole-branch review.
- MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE (k8s-acceptance-loop): on `kind-ax-next-dev`, new chat → send → kill the
  serving pod mid-turn → assert the spinner flips to a red error row with a "retry" button
  (not an infinite spinner); retry re-sends and answers. Host-side TS change rides the fast
  hostPath dist loop (no image rebuild) EXCEPT confirm the runner still fires session:terminate
  on pod death (it already does). Add as a `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` scenario.

lines: +  // chat:turn-error — the terminated-turn terminator (Fault A). The
+  // orchestrator fires this with the ORIGINAL agent:invoke ctx.reqId when a
+  // turn ends abnormally (sandbox death / wedged-runner timeout) instead of
+  // firing chat:turn-end. The SSE handler matches by reqId (NOT
+  // conversationId — the orchestrator carries the original reqId, so the
+  // precise per-turn join key is available) and emits an `error` frame +
+  // closes, so the client flips out of the "Thinking…" spinner.
+  it('turn-error: when chat:turn-error fires with the matching reqId, SSE emits an error frame and closes', async () => {
+    const { bus, initCtx, handler, buffer } = bootHandler();
+  it('turn-error with a non-matching reqId does not close us', async () => {
+    const { bus, initCtx, handler, buffer } = bootHandler();

content.content:
54	
55	/**
56	 * Frame the SSE handler emits to the client. Matches the `data:` JSON the
57	 * browser parses verbatim. The three variants are deliberately disjoint
58	 * so the client can `switch` on a single discriminator.
59	 *
60	 *   - chunk frame: `{ reqId, text, kind }`        — content delta.
61	 *   - phase frame: `{ reqId, phase }`             — out-of-band agent state.
62	 *   - done  frame: `{ reqId, done: true }`         — turn terminator.
63	 */
64	export type SseFrame =
65	  | StreamChunk
66	  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
67	  | { reqId: string; done: true };

input.new_string:
*   - chunk frame: `{ reqId, text, kind }`        — content delta.
 *   - phase frame: `{ reqId, phase }`             — out-of-band agent state.
 *   - done  frame: `{ reqId, done: true }`         — normal turn terminator.
 *   - error frame: `{ reqId, error }`             — abnormal turn terminator.
 *
 * The `error` frame closes the stream when a turn ends WITHOUT a normal
 * `chat:turn-end` (the runner died mid-turn or wedged past the chat
 * timeout). `error` is a stable, backend-agnostic reason code (e.g.
 * `sandbox-terminated`, `chat-run-timeout`) — NOT a pod name / exit code;
 * the client maps it to a user-facing label. Without it the client's
 * "Thinking…" spinner hangs forever (the 25 s keepalive keeps the
 * connection open).
 */
export type SseFrame =
  | StreamChunk
  | { reqId: string; phase: PhaseKind }
  | { reqId: string; done: true }
  | { reqId: string; error: string };

lines: +  // Fault A — signal the channel SSE that a turn ended abnormally (the
+  // runner died mid-turn or wedged past the chat timeout) so the client
+  // flips out of the "Thinking…" spinner into an error+retry state. Without
+  // this the SSE only ever gets a terminal frame on a NORMAL chat:turn-end;
+  // a terminated turn fires chat:end (audit) but no turn-end, so the stream
+  // hangs forever (the 25 s SSE keepalive keeps it open). The only
+  // subscriber today is @ax/channel-web's per-connection SSE handler, which
+  // matches by reqId — so the reqId must be the originating agent:invoke
+  // reqId. Observation-only broadcast; a no-op when no SSE is attached.
+  // Fault A (routed/warm path) — a sandbox that dies mid-turn re-broadcasts
+  // session:terminate (the session store fires it after the teardown service
+  // work; see session-postgres/session-inmemory). The fresh-spawn path
+  // catches death promptly via handle.exited, but the routed path does NOT
+  // watch exited (the handle isn't ours) — it would otherwise hang until the
+  // 10-min chatTimeoutMs. So surface the error promptly for any in-flight
+  // We do NOT resolve/reject the deferred here: the existing bounded-timeout
+  // path still produces the single chat:end (audit invariant). The SSE
+  // closes on the first error frame, so the later duplicate turn-error from
+  // that timeout path is a harmless no-op. Completed turns whose waiter is
+  // already settled (or unregistered) are skipped — no spurious error.
+    // Snapshot — fireTurnError must not be confused by concurrent mutation
+    // of the live Set during the await.

file.content:
## Scenario: Python `pip install` in the sandbox

### What this proves

A session-scoped Python venv is active in the runner, so an agent can
`pip install <pkg>` and then `import <pkg>` in a script — the path a model
reaches for instinctively. The install flows through the credential-proxy
(MITM CA trusted via `PIP_CERT`) and is bounded by the egress allowlist.

### Prerequisites

- A live `ax-next-dev` (kind) cluster on the branch image.
- An agent whose manifest allows a pypi package (e.g. `requests` via
  `capabilities.packages.pypi`).

### Walk

In a chat against that agent, ask it to:

1. Run `pip install requests` in a Bash tool.
   - Expect: install completes with no TLS error. (No TLS error proves
     `PIP_CERT` makes pip trust the proxy MITM CA — the part unit tests
     cannot exercise.)
2. Run `python -c "import requests; print(requests.__version__)"`.
   - Expect: a version string prints (the venv python imports the install).
3. Ask it to `pip install <a NON-allowlisted package>`.
   - Expect: the install is blocked at the proxy (egress allowlist holds).

### Acceptance criteria

- Allowlisted install + import succeed; non-allowlisted install is blocked.
- Nothing the agent installed appears in the next session (ephemeral venv).

input.new_string:
### Acceptance criteria

- Allowlisted install + import succeed; non-allowlisted install is blocked.
- Nothing the agent installed appears in the next session (ephemeral venv).

## Scenario: sandbox death mid-turn → error+retry (Fault A)

### What this proves

When the serving runner dies mid-turn, the host emits a `chat:turn-error`
on the SSE so the client's "Thinking…" spinner flips to a visible error
with a **retry** button — instead of hanging indefinitely (the bug
chat-qa-sweep found: the SSE got no `done`/error frame, so the spinner +
Stop persisted ~indefinitely, recoverable only by manual Stop).

This is a HOST-side TypeScript change, so it rides the fast hostPath dist
loop (no image rebuild). The runner already fires `session:terminate` on
pod death — that's the signal the host keys off.

### Prerequisites

- A live `ax-next-dev` (kind) cluster on the branch dist (channel-web +
  chat-orchestrator).
- The chat UI open in a browser (Playwright MCP or manual).

### Walk

1. Start a new chat and send a message that takes a few seconds (e.g. "write
   a 300-word story"). While the "Thinking…" spinner is showing:
2. Kill the serving runner pod:
   `kubectl -n ax-next-runners delete pod <serving-pod> --now`
   (find it: `kubectl -n ax-next-runners get pods`).
3. Within a second or two, observe the `AgentStatus` row flip from
   "Thinking…" (blue, breathing) to a **red error row** reading "The agent
   stopped unexpectedly. Retry to continue." with a **retry** button — NOT
   a hung spinner. Host logs show `pod_exited` + `pod_killed`.
4. Click **retry**. A fresh sandbox spawns, replays history, and answers —
   the turn completes normally.
5. (Warm-reuse variant) Send a second message on the same conversation so it
   routes into the warm session, then kill the pod again mid-turn. Same
   prompt error+retry should appear promptly (the `session:terminate`
   subscriber covers the routed path — without it this case would hang until
   the 10-min chat timeout).

### Acceptance criteria

- The spinner flips to a red error+retry row within ~1-2 s of the pod kill,
  on BOTH the fresh-spawn (turn 1) and warm-reuse (turn 2+) paths.
- Retry re-sends and the next turn completes; subsequent normal messages
  work.
- No infinite spinner, no white screen.

### Prerequisites

git add packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts packages/chat-orchestrator/src/plugin.ts packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts && \
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(chat-orchestrator): fire chat:turn-error on abnormal turn end (Fault A)

When a turn ends abnormally — the runner dies mid-turn or wedges past
chatTimeoutMs — the orchestrator fired chat:end (audit) but never
chat:turn-end, so the channel SSE got no terminal frame and the client's
"Thinking…" spinner hung forever (the 25s SSE keepalive keeps the stream
open). chat-qa-sweep flagged this as a ship-blocker (Fault A).

Fire a new broadcast `chat:turn-error {reqId, reason}` (carrying the
originating agent:invoke reqId, the SSE join key) at two points:

- the synthesized-terminated chokepoints in BOTH the fresh-spawn and
  routed paths — covers sandbox-exit + the wedged-runner timeout.
- a new `session:terminate` subscriber — covers the routed/warm path
  (which doesn't watch handle.exited) PROMPTLY, instead of waiting the
  full 10-min chatTimeoutMs. Guarded on `!deferred.settled` so completed
  turns don't fire a spurious error.

The subscriber does not resolve the deferred: the existing bounded-timeout
path still produces the single chat:end (audit invariant); a later
duplicate turn-error is a harmless no-op (the SSE closes on the first).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "--- commit 1 done ---" && git log --oneline -1

git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(channel-web): emit SSE error frame on chat:turn-error (Fault A)

The SSE handler only ever closed a stream on chat:turn-end (the done
frame). Subscribe (per-connection) to the orchestrator's new
chat:turn-error, matched by reqId, and emit a new SseFrame `{reqId,
error}` terminator + close — so a turn that died abnormally stops hanging
the client. `error` is a backend-agnostic reason code (e.g.
sandbox-terminated, chat-run-timeout), not a pod name / exit code.

A boot-time turn-error evictor (reusing the turn-end evictor) drops
buffered chunks for an errored turn with no SSE listener attached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "--- commit 2 done ---" && git log --oneline -1

git add packages/channel-web/src/lib/transport.ts packages/channel-web/src/lib/runtime.tsx packages/channel-web/src/lib/turn-error.ts packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/transport.test.ts packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/turn-error.test.ts && \
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(channel-web): flip AgentStatus to error+retry on a turn-error frame (Fault A)

Client side of the Fault A fix. The transport parses the new `{reqId,
error}` SSE frame, closes any open part, and emits an AI-SDK `error`
UIMessageChunk — terminating the turn as ERRORED (running flips false)
instead of a silent finish or an indefinite spinner. A reason→label map
(client-owned, mirroring PHASE_LABELS) renders a friendly message.

The runtime wires `useChat({ onError })` → `applyTurnError`, flipping the
agent-status row to error mode with a **retry** that calls `regenerate()`
(re-runs the last user turn against a fresh sandbox; history is
server-side and the dead session's active_session_id was cleared by
session:terminate). RunningEffect's unmount only hides a `working` row,
so the error row persists. `agentStatusActions.error` existed but was
previously wired to nothing in production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "--- commit 3 done ---" && git log --oneline -1

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head worktree-fault-a-sse-turn-error --title "fix: sandbox death mid-turn surfaces error+retry instead of a hung spinner (Fault A)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What & why

chat-qa-sweep flagged a **ship-blocker** (Fault A): kill the serving runner pod mid-turn and the host logs `pod_exited code:143` + `pod_killed`, but the client SSE (`/api/chat/stream/:reqId`, HTTP 200) never receives an error/`done` frame → the `AgentStatus` "Thinking…" spinner + Stop persist **indefinitely** (recoverable only by manual Stop).

**Root cause:** the SSE handler only emits a terminal frame on `chat:turn-end`. On a *terminated* outcome the orchestrator fires `chat:end` (audit) but never `chat:turn-end`, so the stream gets no terminator and the 25 s keepalive holds it open forever.

## The fix (one mechanism, end-to-end)

A new broadcast **`chat:turn-error {reqId, reason}`** fired by the orchestrator on abnormal turn end, consumed by a per-connection SSE subscriber → new `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}` + close. The client transport turns that into an AI-SDK `error` chunk; the runtime's `onError` flips `AgentStatus` to **error + retry** (`regenerate()`).

Fired at two complementary points so it's robust across the keepalive-default k8s deployment:
- **synthesized-terminated chokepoints** (fresh-spawn + routed) — covers sandbox-exit and the wedged-runner timeout.
- **a new `session:terminate` subscriber** — covers the routed/warm path (which doesn't watch `handle.exited`) **promptly**, instead of waiting the full 10-min `chatTimeoutMs`.

**Scope:** Fault A (sandbox death) + the closely-related wedged-runner timeout. Faults **B/D** (host bounce / network drop → silent finalize) are a **separate** finding with a different root cause (the transport synthesizes a finish on a done-less close) — left as their own TODO item, not folded in here.

## Boundary review (new `chat:turn-error` hook)

- **Alternate impl:** a CLI channel would subscribe to render a terminal error line; a metrics plugin could count failed turns. Genuine broadcast with multiple potential subscribers (mirrors `chat:phase` / `chat:turn-end`).
- **Payload field names that might leak:** `reqId` (server-minted correlation id, already the join key for `chat:stream-chunk`/`chat:phase`) and `reason` (a stable, backend-agnostic code — `sandbox-exit-before-chat-end`, `chat-run-timeout`, `sandbox-terminated`, `chat-run-error` — orchestrator vocabulary, **not** pod name / exit code / sha). None leak.
- **Subscriber risk:** no backend-specific field; `reason` is safe across the k8s/subprocess impls.
- **Wire surface:** `chat:turn-error` is host-internal (orchestrator→channel-web), **not** IPC — it doesn't cross the runner⇄host boundary. The browser-facing `SseFrame` `{reqId, error}` schema lives in channel-web's own `types.ts`.

## Security note

No sandbox boundary / IPC transport / plugin-loading / dependency change. `reason` is **host-orchestrator-generated** (a fixed code set), not model/tool/user output — not untrusted content. The new SSE `error` field renders as escaped React text (`agentStatusActions.error` → text node), no XSS. (security-checklist skill triggers don't fire.)

## Tests (Bug Fix Policy)

RED→GREEN regression tests across the chain:
- **orchestrator** — fresh-spawn sandbox-exit / wedged timeout / in-flight `session:terminate` each fire `chat:turn-error` with the originating reqId; no-waiter `session:terminate` and a completed turn do **not** fire.
- **SSE handler** — `chat:turn-error` for the connection's reqId emits an `error` frame + closes; a non-matching reqId is ignored.
- **transport** — an `error` frame closes any open part and emits an AI-SDK `error` chunk (terminates, no silent finish); reason→label mapping.
- **runtime helper** (`applyTurnError`) — flips the status row to error+retry; falls back to a default message.

Gate: `pnpm build` (tsc clean) + full `pnpm -r run test` (all packages green) + `pnpm test:eslint-rules` + eslint on changed files.

## Deferred

- **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE live walk on `kind-ax-next-dev`** (added as a scenario in `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`): kill the serving pod mid-turn, confirm the spinner flips to error+retry within ~1-2 s and retry re-answers, on both the fresh-spawn and warm-reuse paths. Host-side TS, so it rides the fast hostPath dist loop (no image rebuild). Not yet walked.
- Routed/warm mid-turn death surfaces the SSE error promptly via `session:terminate`, but the in-flight waiter still lingers until `chatTimeoutMs` (the existing, documented routed behavior — `chat:end` still fires once via the timeout). No functional impact; not changed here.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

2026-05-23 yolo-ship of the chat-qa-sweep **Fault A ship-blocker** (see
[[project_chat_qa_sweep_2026_05_23]]). Branch `worktree-fault-a-sse-turn-error`
(isolated worktree off origin/main), **PR #137**, base main. Green + lint-clean
locally; ship loop (CodeRabbit/CI) in progress at time of writing — NOT merged.

**Bug:** kill the serving runner pod mid-turn → host logs `pod_exited code:143`
but the client SSE got no error/`done` frame → "Thinking…" spinner hung forever
(the 25s keepalive holds the stream open). Root cause: the SSE handler only emits
a terminal frame on `chat:turn-end`; a *terminated* outcome fires `chat:end`
(audit) but never `chat:turn-end`.

**Fix (one mechanism, end-to-end):** new broadcast **`chat:turn-error {reqId,
reason}`** fired by the orchestrator → per-connection SSE subscriber (match by
**reqId**, unlike turn-end which matches conversationId — the orchestrator
carries the original agent:invoke reqId) → new `SseFrame {reqId, error}` + close.
Client transport emits an AI-SDK `{type:'error', errorText}` chunk (terminates
turn, `running`→false); runtime `useChat({onError})` → `applyTurnError` flips
`agentStatusActions` to error + **retry**=`regenerate()`. `agentStatusActions.error`
existed but was wired to NOTHING in prod before this.

**Two fire points** (robust across keepalive-default k8s): (a) the
`if(!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` chokepoints in BOTH fresh-spawn + routed
paths (sandbox-exit + wedged-runner timeout); (b) a new **`session:terminate`
subscriber** firing turn-error for in-flight `!settled` reqIds — covers the
routed/warm path PROMPTLY (it doesn't watch handle.exited; else it'd hang until
the 10-min chatTimeoutMs). The subscriber does NOT resolve the deferred — the
existing timeout path still fires the single chat:end (audit invariant); a later
dup turn-error is a no-op (SSE already closed).

**Scope:** Fault A + wedged-timeout only. Faults **B/D** (host bounce / net drop
→ silent finalize) are a SEPARATE finding (transport `flush()` synthesizes a
done-less finish) — left as their own TODO, not folded in.

**Files:** chat-orchestrator (orchestrator.ts fireTurnError + onSessionTerminate;
plugin.ts subscribes session:terminate), channel-web server (types.ts SseFrame
error variant, sse.ts per-conn subscriber, plugin.ts subscribes + evictor),
channel-web client (transport.ts ERROR_LABELS+parse, runtime.tsx onError, new
turn-error.ts helper). +2 new files. RED→GREEN tests in all 4 areas.

**Deferred:** MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE live walk on kind (added as a scenario in
deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md) — host-side TS so it rides the fast hostPath dist
loop, no image rebuild; not yet walked.

**Gotcha hit this run:** my first test Edit used a `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/...`
absolute path = the MAIN checkout, NOT the worktree (`.../.claude/worktrees/
fault-a-sse-turn-error/...`). Reverted main with `git checkout --`, re-applied to
the worktree. decisions.md DOES belong in the main checkout (canonical). Lesson:
in a worktree, code edits MUST use the worktree path prefix.

content.text:
Implementation is complete and pushed as **PR #137**. Here's where things stand:

## Summary

**Fault A — sandbox death mid-turn → permanent silent hung spinner** is fixed end-to-end.

**Root cause:** the SSE handler only emits a terminal frame on `chat:turn-end`. A *terminated* outcome fires `chat:end` (audit) but never `chat:turn-end`, so the stream got no terminator and the 25 s keepalive held it open forever.

**The fix (one mechanism):**
- **Orchestrator** fires a new `chat:turn-error {reqId, reason}` broadcast on abnormal turn end — at the terminated-outcome chokepoints (fresh-spawn + routed paths, covering sandbox-exit + wedged-runner timeout) **and** via a new `session:terminate` subscriber that covers the routed/warm path *promptly* (it doesn't watch `handle.exited`; otherwise it'd hang until the 10-min timeout).
- **Channel-web SSE** consumes it (per-connection, matched by reqId) → new `{reqId, error}` frame + close.
- **Client transport** turns that into an AI-SDK `error` chunk (turn ends, spinner stops); the **runtime's `onError`** flips `AgentStatus` to **error + retry** (`regenerate()`).

**Verification:** RED→GREEN regression tests in all four areas; `pnpm build` (tsc clean) + full `pnpm -r run test` (every package green) + `pnpm test:eslint-rules` + eslint on changed files — all green. 4 clean commits, no stray edits.

**Scope:** Fault A + the related wedged-runner timeout. Faults B/D (host bounce / network drop) are a separate finding with a different root cause — deliberately not folded in.

**Deferred:** live MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk on `kind-ax-next-dev` (added as a scenario doc; host-side TS so it rides the fast dist loop, no image rebuild).

I've armed a monitor on PR #137's checks — I'll address CodeRabbit comments and any CI failures as they land, then report back when it's green and review-clean. Note: the Fault A line lives in your unpushed `docs/chat-qa-sweep-findings` branch (not on `main`), so I didn't touch TODO.md to avoid a merge conflict — you can strike it referencing #137 when that branch lands.
